programme.title: Father Dowling Mysteries
programme.desc: The crime-busting clergyman befriends a woman whose mysterious disappearance alerts them to a bizarre kidnapping plot. Sister Steve goes undercover as a maid for the missing woman's family to get to the bottom of the mystery. Starring Tom Bosley and Tracy Nelson

programme.title: Father Dowling Mysteries
programme.desc: Sister Steve protects a boy from a killer masquerading as a department-store Santa, while Father Dowling tries to convince police the threat is serious. Tom Bosley and Tracy Nelson star

programme.title: Perry Mason

programme.title: CSI: Miami
programme.desc: The murder of a cigar-maker leads to some surprising facts being uncovered about the victim. The team's enquiries turn up a surprising connection to Cuba, Fidel Castro and a hidden, bloody past. programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Vance insists on taking charge of an investigation into the fatal shooting of a boxer - but antagonises Gibbs in the process, and finds his own past coming under scrutiny as the case unfolds. Drama, starring Mark Harmon and Rocky Carroll

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: A man who stole a car and ran down and killed one of his pursuers before escaping is identified as Walton Flynn, an ex-marine now working for a private security firm in Iraq. Sam and Callen learn that the businessman he had been hired to protect had been murdered and that the people chasing him were hired by his employers to dish out their own form of justice. Crime drama, starring Chris O'Donnell and LL Cool J

programme.title: The Fugitive

programme.title: Perry Mason

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Part one of two. First lieutenant Michael Torres gets involved in a fight before falling to the ground, and is found beaten to death the next morning. programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: A jet-skier crashes on to the shore and is discovered to be dead, having been shot through the chest. Sam and Callen visit the victim's apartment, where they find the body of a woman on the floor

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Tony and Ziva visit Paris to escort Nora Williams, the key witness in an embezzlement case, back to America - but on the return flight they realise an assassin has boarded the plane with them, determined to ensure she never reaches home soil to testify. programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Marine Brandon Valdivia falls to his death from a rooftop bar and is initially thought to have committed suicide, but his family refuse to accept that possibility and persuade a congressman to put some pressure on the admiral. Sam and Callen are then brought in to re-evaluate the case and discover that Valdivia may have been involved in money laundering with a fellow marine.

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Tony and Ziva track a suspected terrorist to a warehouse, where they find traces of radioactive metal - together with evidence suggesting a nuclear attack is being planned on Washington, DC. programme.title: Bondi Rescue

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Dr Sloan investigates a fatal car accident, and is baffled when he discovers the dead racing driver had survived a similar mishap only days earlier. Medical detective drama, starring Dick Van Dyke

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: The death of a four-year-old girl forces Dr Sloan to choose between hospital protocol and honour, casting a shadow over his entire career. Drama, starring Dick Van Dyke

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: Sylvester seriously injures himself after triggering an explosive device during an investigation into a gang of thieves. The team must work with Interpol and remain focused on finding the culprits while their friend is in a critical condition. Meanwhile, Drew decides he wants to get close to Paige again. Drama, inspired by a true story, starring Ari Stidham and Brendan Hines

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: The team races against the clock to save a boy who is trapped inside a sinkhole. Walter manages to get Megan into a study researching a new MS drug, but she is not keen on being part of a trial. Meanwhile, Paige tries to bring the festive spirit to the team when Drew is forced to miss Christmas with Ralph for a baseball try-out in Portland. Drama, starring Elyes Gabel, Eddie Kaye Thomas and Camille Guaty

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: While on a joint Coast Guard and NCIS training exercise at sea with Agent Borin, Gibbs spots a dead man overboard, who is found to be a missing member of the crew of a nearby vessel. However, when the agent boards the ship for questioning, he realises it has been commandeered by pirates. Naval drama, starring Mark Harmon, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team investigates the murder of a Navy commander's wife, determining whether it is linked to her role as a therapist, or an act of terrorism since her husband's name has appeared on a Jihadist's target list. Elsewhere, the group discusses Halloween costumes, as well as Tony's recent dating habits. Starring Mark Harmon, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team uncovers a fraudulent charity that preys on those searching for missing military personnel while investigating the murder of a retired Master Sergeant. Meanwhile, Bishop agonises over the results of her probation evaluation, and Tony contemplates adopting a stray cat that followed him home. Starring Mark Harmon, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team investigates the cause of a hit-and-run incident in which a Petty Officer has been killed. Meanwhile, Gibbs works to clear a Navy Hospital Corpsman who has been indicted for illegally providing medical aid to victims of the crash, despite the injured insisting her techniques helped save their lives. Starring Mark Harmon, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: An enigmatic killer shoots dead a businessman spending Thanksgiving at a hotel in the city, then takes his glasses, jewellery and ID to Washington Dulles airport, which has been brought to a stand-still by a massive snowstorm. As the murderer's actions take effect, a terror alert is issued, leaving all northern US airports in a state of panic, and leaving it up to DiNozzo, Bishop and her husband - all of whom are trapped at Dulles by the weather - to bring the armed villain to justice. Crime drama, starring Michael Weatherley

Meanwhile, the team is called in to investigate the death of a sergeant from carbon monoxide poisoning - and finds a copy of Ducky's exhumation request on the man's laptop. Crime drama, starring David McCallum

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team is called in to investigate when three dehydrated survivors from a helicopter crash are discovered. The pilot is initially missing, but his body later washes ashore with a gunshot wound to the head. Things get complicated when one of the crew members tells the agents the chopper was hit by another aircraft, while another states that the pilot tried to kill them all by crashing into the sea on purpose. American drama, starring Mark Harmon and David McCallum

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The driver of a Ferrari is gunned down by a motorcyclist and the victim is identified as petty officer Colin Boxer, who was holding down a second job as a valet parker and had borrowed the sports car. The owner of the vehicle turns out to be Vijay Chaya, the billionaire head of a large corporation, and the team suspects a case of mistaken identity. During the course of the investigation, Gibbs chances on a medal awarded to Leroy Jethro Moore, the man he was named after and an estranged friend of his father. Guest starring Billy Dee Williams (The Empire Strikes Back) and Ralph Waite (The Waltons)

programme.desc: Part three of three. programme.title: Casualty

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Mel's younger brother and his girlfriend come to stay on New Year's Eve and she goes to work thinking the couple are happily celebrating in Trafalgar Square - until Sun Hill officers are called to investigate a stabbing in a club. With the troublesome youngster in the frame for the attack, apparently provoked by a fellow clubber who made advances to his companion, Mel sets out to persuade her colleagues he is innocent

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Ricky is stunned by Bianca's news. Grant tries to make peace with Peggy. Irene discovers her maternal instincts. A face from Tony's past comes back to haunt him

programme.desc: Feature-length episode. As Dalziel takes it easy at a local B&B after a bout of ill-health, Pascoe returns to his home town to attend the wedding of an old friend. However, the ceremony ends abruptly when a man is found beaten to death. Andy joins his colleague to investigate - but the duo become rapidly embroiled in a plot of intrigue and murder that implicates Peter's father. Warren Clarke, Colin Buchanan, John McEnery and Jim Carter star

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Gerry is forced to confront his past when the team reinvestigates the 33-year-old murder of a doctor whose body was found hanging from a meat hook on a butcher's stall at Smithfield Market. The butcher had gone missing on the same day, and since then the community has rallied to help his widow keep the business afloat. However, his recently discovered remains reveal a match to an unidentified person and lead to a shocking revelation for Sandra. Guest starring Ken Farrington, David Calder and Diana Quick, with Amanda Redman and Alun Armstrong

programme.title: Trial & Retribution
programme.desc: Fifteen-year-old Cassie Booth goes missing while doing her local paper round, and her blood-stained clothes are later found in a nearby boathouse owned by Karl Wilding. He is the prime suspect, but then seemingly helpful Stephen Warrington, a wine merchant who has initially brought a complaint about a house being used as a brothel, demonstrates too much interest in the case

programme.title: The Bill

With the troublesome youngster in the frame for the attack, apparently provoked by a fellow clubber who made advances to his companion, Mel sets out to persuade her colleagues he is innocent

Knowing the problem will escalate as time goes by, the detective becomes determined to nip the violence in the bud, so he, Gene and Annie go undercover in a pub to catch the culprits. However, he realises the derby match that will be the focus of the conflict is the last one he went to with his dad when he was four years old

programme.title: Casualty

programme.desc: When a young mother is found badly beaten in an alleyway, Sun Hill CID is drawn into her tale of debt, blackmail and a complex love life. Christopher Timothy and Duncan James guest star

programme.desc: Phil is sleeping rough and is back on the bottle; Grant and Peggy demand some answers from him. Simon and Tiffany go to hospital to visit their father

programme.desc: Pat quizzes Barry about the wedding. Tiffany's dad phones; he is now homeless. At the sports hall opening ceremony, George takes Councillor Dixon for a drive and a chat

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Tracey argues with Darryl about the lack of trust in their relationship, and visits a therapist for advice on how to resolve the issue. Comedy, starring Linda Robson

programme.desc: Joe Maplin hatches a hare-brained scheme to stop a hospital being built next to the camp site, ordering staff to make a din from dawn till dusk while the council committee conduct an inspection visit. Jimmy Perry and David Croft's comedy, starring Ruth Madoc, Paul Shane and Su Pollard

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Hyacinth disappoints Richard by buying him a pair of skis for his birthday, intending to strap them to the car in yet another attempt to impress the neighbours. However, the scheme backfires when her rich sister Violet arrives and cuts her down to size

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Billy seems to have gained a stalker, but soon realises he has greater worries after discovering he promised to demonstrate archery at the vicar's garden party - while riding a horse. Comedy, starring Peter Sallis and Keith Clifford

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: A man suspected of killing a police officer is seen near Kembleford and Mallory is determined to catch him, but he has to ask the sleuthing priest for help when disaster strikes

programme.title: Happy Valley
programme.desc: Sally Wainwright's crime drama picks up 18 months after the events of series one. While out solving a case of sheep rustling, Catherine discovers a decomposed body hidden in a garage on a local estate, and soon finds herself in the middle of a murder investigation. Tommy, now serving life in a high-security prison, is given some unexpected news and seeks comfort in a mysterious female visitor, while Detective Sergeant John Wadsworth faces a tough decision when his girlfriend unexpectedly turns up at the house he shares with his wife and children. Sarah Lancashire stars

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The team reinvestigates the death of wealthy financier Douglas Anderson when psychic Sebastian Carter informs the deceased's daughter that she needs to resolve her father's unfinished business. The retired officers set out to prove the clairvoyant is a fraud after growing suspicious of his 'gift', but Pullman struggles to dismiss the supposed conman when a conflict involving her own late father emerges. Detective drama, guest starring David Bradley

programme.title: Trial & Retribution
programme.desc: Pat is forced to deal with a persistent complainer who has been hounding the station, and becomes concerned when he starts stalking her. Eventually Pat is abducted by Warrington and forced to discover Cassie North's corpse. Drama, starring Kate Buffery and Richard E Grant

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: When a young mother is found badly beaten in an alleyway, Sun Hill CID is drawn into her tale of debt, blackmail and a complex love life. Christopher Timothy and Duncan James guest star

programme.title: Life on Mars
programme.desc: Sam hears his mother's voice saying goodbye, and worries that doctors in 2006 are about to turn off his life support system. When a hostage situation crops up, he becomes convinced that resolving the crisis without a single casualty will cause him to wake up in his own time. However, Gene is determined to go in with all guns blazing - putting everyone's safety at risk. John Simm stars

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.desc: A penniless sculptor seeks the canny dealer's help to prise a considerable inheritance out of his avaricious brother. Celia Imrie guest stars, with Ian McShane

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.desc: The police suspect Lovejoy of stealing from one of Jane's old friends, forcing him to enlist the help of refuse collector extraordinaire Brian Nun to clear himself. programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.desc: Two mysterious murders appear to be the work of an ancient Mayan curse attached to a statue acquired by Lovejoy. Drama, starring Ian McShane and Phyllis Logan

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.desc: The dodgy dealer is asked to furnish a restaurant, but the items he acquires turn out to be stolen. To make matters worse, the owners turn up for the establishment's opening night. John Wells guest stars, with Ian McShane

programme.title: Sharpe
programme.desc: Richard Sharpe and the faithful Harper begin their latest tour of duty by uncovering top-level corruption in the Army. Reporting their findings leads to attempts on their lives, so they fake their own deaths and re-enlist under new identities in an attempt to expose the culprits. Guest starring Julian Fellowes and featuring Alexander Armstrong in an early role

programme.title: Rosemary & Thyme
programme.desc: Laura and Rosemary are hired to re-create a Victorian garden in Regent's Park, where they meet all manner of intriguing characters - but the oasis of calm in central London is shattered by the discovery of a body. The victim is a respected Harley Street psychiatrist - whose three-legged dog appears to hold the key to solving the case. Mystery drama, starring Felicity Kendal and Pam Ferris

programme.title: Rosemary & Thyme
programme.desc: The duo are hired to create a garden as a wedding present, and find a baby under a gooseberry bush. The discovery brings out Rosemary's maternal side as the pair investigate who abandoned the child. Their task is complicated by the murder of an artistic neighbour - and the two mysterious incidents appear to be connected. Starring Felicity Kendal and Pam Ferris

programme.title: Rosemary & Thyme
programme.desc: The duo visit a resort in Spain to create a trophy garden for a pro-am tennis tournament run by a former champion. When one of the competitors is found dead, everyone assumes it to be a gruesome accident - but there is more to the case than meets the eye. While investigating, Laura enters the mysterious world of bullfighting, and Rosemary rediscovers her love of motor scooters

programme.title: Rosemary & Thyme
programme.desc: The green-fingered amateur detectives work on a garden for an open-air theatre, where Rosemary's cousin is due to perform. However, the production suffers a setback when an actor shoots himself during rehearsal - and the gun is found to have contained live bullets instead of blanks. A member of the cast gets an attack of stage fright, and Laura is obliged to take her place in the play as the duo try to find the culprit. Drama, with Felicity Kendal and Pam Ferris

programme.title: The Brokenwood Mysteries
programme.desc: During a performance of Hamlet, young cast member Ben Faulkner collapses and dies. Initially the detectives think Ben died of natural causes, until Mike uncovers a complex web of ego-driven dissension among the troupe and follows his nose to the real cause of death

programme.title: Shakespeare & Hathaway - Private Investigators
programme.desc: Frank and Lu investigate a death threat at Runningbrook, an exclusive tennis club that hides a toxic threat. Crime drama, starring Mark Benton and Jo Joyner

programme.title: Rebus
programme.desc: While Rebus looks into the apparent suicide of a man at an investment bank, Clarke investigates a stabbing on the seedy Westmills estate. The inspector learns his victim had links to a chemical company building a new plant in the area, as well as an Angolan village where children have been used unscrupulously in drug testing - but before his latest informant can pass on vital evidence, she too is silenced. Drama, starring Ken Stott and Claire Price

programme.title: Wallander
programme.desc: Kenneth Branagh returns to his Bafta-winning role of the Swedish detective in three adaptations of stories by Henning Mankell. Wallander moves into a new home with his girlfriend, only for their dreams of domestic bliss to be shattered when they discover a skeleton in the garden. He must find the killer if he has any hopes of getting his life settled again - and is surprised to discover the body was buried more recently than he first thought. Saskia Reeves and Sarah Smart co-star

programme.title: Judge John Deed
programme.desc: Part one of two. Deed reluctantly helps Jo by examining why funding for legal aid was withdrawn in the case of a soldier who committed suicide after being damaged by army vaccines. However, he risks causing a constitutional crisis when he decides the only way to win justice for the deceased's family is to accuse the original judge of bias

programme.title: Judge John Deed
programme.desc: Part two of two. Deed's investigation into why a soldier's legal funding to sue a pharmaceutical company was withdrawn leads to more sinister discoveries. Forces begin to work against him with shocking consequences. Legal drama, starring Martin Shaw, Jenny Seagrove and Donald Sinden

programme.title: Life on Mars
programme.desc: A drug dealer dies while in police custody and Sam suspects a cover-up within the unit. He orders a full investigation, though it seems none of the other officers is prepared to help. To Gene's relief, the pathologist's report appears to put everyone in the clear, but when the truth about the criminal's demise emerges, it is up to the 21st-century cop to decide whether to reveal it. John Simm and Philip Glenister star

programme.desc: Wales v Denmark (Kick-off 5.00pm). Coverage of the opening match in the last 16, which takes place at Johan Cruijff ArenA in Amsterdam. Denmark seemed to be heading for an early exit after losing their opening two fixtures against Finland and Belgium, but a remarkable 4-1 thumping of Russia, and other results going their way, saw them progress as Group B runners-up

To realise their dreams of a stylish social garden, the duo call upon the services of Charlie Dimmock and the David and Harry Rich to perform the transformation they need on a budget of £4,000

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Dealers Ian Humphries, Jane Cave, Adrian Higham, James Gooch, Natasha Francis and Tracy Martin go head to head over a polychrome carving of St Sebastian, a pair of vintage Bakelite phones, some Chinese fortune sticks and a piece of rock 'n' roll history. Presented by Nigel Havers

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Ronan Keating host the live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: The discovery of a double crime prompts Sonia to point the finger of suspicion at Rocky. Bobby decides to come clean to Dana, while Ruby and Martin receive another setback. Suki is furious with Kheerat, so he tells Sharon it's over for the sake of his family. But Suki's anger turns to upset when she hears some shocking news

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Chelsea lands herself in hot water with the police, and when Jack suggests she gets herself a lawyer, she asks Gray out for lunch - and it isn't long before the two are flirting. Isaac's obsession continues to build, Ben opens up to Callum and Peter encourages Bobby to fight for Dana

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Jo Brand, Simon Day, Kiri Pritchard-McLean and Henning Wehn join host Rob Brydon and regular captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show. As usual, the participants set out to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible fibs about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory, through rounds including Home Truths and Quick-fire Lies

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: Theo Paphitis steps in for Peter Jones, joining regular Dragons Deborah Meaden, Touker Suleyman, Tej Lalvani and Sara Davies to assess more money-making schemes. A musical inventor shows off his drumming training device, and four children make a first aid demonstration to secure investment for their parents' business. Plus, pitches for culinary alcohol and a prototype shoe mechanism

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce introduces the topical debate, with a virtual audience from around the UK. This week's panellists are Secretary of State for Justice and Lord Chancellor Robert Buckland MP, Shadow Health and Social Care Secretary Jonathan Ashworth MP, Green Party MP Caroline Lucas, Chair of the NHS Confederation Victor Adebowale and Economics correspondent at the Spectator Kate Andrews

programme.title: Newscast
programme.desc: Adam Fleming, Laura Kuenssberg and Chris Mason present their weekly round-up from Westminster, delivering their usual mix of serious analysis and light-hearted gossip about the biggest stories in politics

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell follow the development progress of auction-bought properties in Baddeley Green in Stoke, Rushden in Northampton and the village of Cwm near Ebbw Vale in Blaenau Gwent

programme.title: Rip Off Britain: Holidays
programme.desc: The travellers still fighting for refunds months after their holidays were cancelled, and a couple asked to pay out for a transatlantic flight to a cruise that never took place. Plus, the story of the customers given false hope after an insolvent coach holiday company was brought back to life by new owners. Presented by Angela Rippon, Gloria Hunniford and Julia Somerville

programme.title: The Moment of Proof
programme.desc: How a discarded gun cartridge assisted police in convicting a man who had gunned down a fellow officer. Plus, the packet of medication that helped lock up a gang of violent jewellery thieves. Narrated by Chuku Modu

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross looks at buying and selling design classics of the past, looking back at some of the best buys from past programmes. As well as revisiting some of the show's favourite experts and auctioneers, Charlie recalls a visit to the Brighton Pavilion where he learned all about its connection with a famous hat designer

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Over three rounds, contestants John, Maria, Caroline and Mark vie to find the single 'unbeatable' answer and streak ahead of the rest to make the final where they can win a cash prize. General knowledge quiz, hosted by Jason Manford

programme.title: Home Is Where the Art Is
programme.desc: Nick Knowles challenges three artists to create a suitable piece to celebrate the upcoming wedding of paraclimbing champion Richard and fiancee Keely. Can a metal sculptor, a stained-glass designer or a paint and textile artist create just what the pair are looking for?

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin heads to the Wye Valley, where he helps an entrepreneurial couple with a £650,000 budget find a dream home which allows them to build a rural business. Along the way he visits the UK's first solar aquaponics greenhouse and discovers how 21st-century technology there could be the sustainable solution to growing enough food to solve the world's food shortage

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: In Malvern, Worcestershire, Miriam and Christopher have a concrete garden, but want one inspired by their time in Indonesia. Charlie Dimmock and brothers Harry and David Rich come up with plans to transform the space on a budget of £3,000

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: The dealers are offered to chance to bid on a mid 20th-century French cabinet used to display a bride's veil, an unusual wooden children's toy and a round metal case for holding needles and shuttles. Presented by Nigel Havers

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Ronan Keating present the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Iqra offers advice to Bobby about lying in relationships, only to make her feel bad about being deceitful herself - so she decides to reveal the truth to Mila. Sheree is shocked by Chelsea's plan to wrap Gray around her little finger so she doesn't have to pay for his legal services. Vinny hits rock bottom and Rainie tells Bernie she is still not sure who she will choose as her surrogate

programme.title: Ant-Man (2015)
programme.desc: A thief attempting to go straight is recruited by a scientist who provides him with a suit that gives him the power to shrink. He must use his new skills to carry out a special mission - helping the inventor to steal back his miniaturisation technology from a ruthless businessman who intends to turn it into a deadly weapon. Superhero adventure, starring Paul Rudd, Michael Douglas and Evangeline Lilly

programme.title: The Producers (1968)
programme.desc: A crooked Broadway producer and his neurotic accountant discover they can make a fortune by putting on a play that closes on opening night, then pocketing the unspent budget. The duo set out to put on the worst show of all time - a tasteless little number called Springtime for Hitler written by a deranged former Nazi. Mel Brooks' comedy, starring Zero Mostel, Gene Wilder and Kenneth Mars

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Sabrina Ghayour and Shivi Ramoutar and special guest Jack Savoretti. Olly Smith joins via video link to pick wines to go with the studio dishes

programme.title: Our Food, Our Family with Michela Chiappa
programme.desc: The Welsh-Italian cook meets four generations of food lovers in Cardiff. While joining the Aslams to rustle up some of their favourite curries, fish dishes and a keema bolognese, Michela meets 90-year-old head of the family Mohammed, who came from Bangladesh in 1958 and went on to open one of Wales' very first Asian restaurants. Michela tries to prise the family's secret spice mix as well as helping grandson Yusuf to master one of his grandmother's specialities to bring along to a special party to celebrate his grandparents' 54th wedding anniversary

programme.title: Tennis: Eastbourne
programme.desc: The Viking International Eastbourne. Clare Balding presents coverage of the women's singles final on day five from Devonshire Park Lawn Tennis Club. Karolina Pliskova of the Czech Republic took the honours when this tournament was last staged in 2019, prevailing 6-1, 6-4 against Germany's 2018 Wimbledon champion Angelique Kerber in the showpiece match

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore sees what people are about to throw away at a Surrey recycling centre, rescuing two car axle stands, a farmhouse table and some rusty old railings for transformation

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Arit Anderson and Harry and David Rich compete as they design gardens for a Swansea couple who want a family space suitable for their two small children. With an ugly concrete outhouse, dangerous broken steps, mismatched levels and wild overgrown corners, the current garden is neither safe not attractive

programme.title: MOTD Live: UEFA Euro 2020
programme.desc: Wales v Denmark (Kick-off 5.00pm). Coverage of the opening match in the last 16, which takes place at Johan Cruijff ArenA in Amsterdam. Wales only won one of their group matches, but that victory against Turkey and their draw against Switzerland was enough to see them finish second behind Italy, who romped through the group stage, winning all three matches without conceding a goal. Denmark seemed to be heading for an early exit after losing their opening two fixtures against Finland and Belgium, but a remarkable 4-1 thumping of Russia, and other results going their way, saw them progress as Group B runners-up

programme.title: MOTD Live: UEFA Euro 2020
programme.desc: Italy v Austria (Kick-off 8.00pm). All the action from the second last-16 fixture, which takes place at Wembley Stadium. The Italians have been one of the outstanding teams in the tournament so far, moving from dark horses to being one of the favourites to lift the trophy. They defeated both Turkey and Switzerland 3-0, before dominating Wales in a 1-0 victory to stretch their winning record to 10 consecutive matches, all of which have been without conceding a single goal. Austria finished second behind the Netherlands in Group C, defeating North Macedonia and Ukraine to progress beyond the opening group phase of a major tournament for the first time since 1982

programme.title: UEFA Euro 2020 Highlights
programme.desc: Wales v Denmark and Italy v Austria. The opening two last-16 matches, held at Johan Cruijff ArenA in Amsterdam and Wembley Stadium, as the first two quarter-finalists were decided

programme.title: Best of Top Gear
programme.desc: Highlights from series 29 and 30, including the time when the presenters were challenged to spend 24 hours in company cars in Paddy McGuinness' home town of Bolton, and Freddie Flintoff racing an Extreme E electric off-roader against a man in a jet pack

programme.title: MOTD: UEFA Euro 2020 Replay
programme.desc: A chance to see one of the opening two last-16 matches, from either Johan Cruijff ArenA in Amsterdam or Wembley Stadium

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Jason Manford hosts the quiz in which contestants aim to find the single 'unbeatable' answer and streak ahead of the rest. Battling it out today are Cole, Charlie, Karen and Charlotte

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Pulp frontman Jarvis Cocker and the Happy Mondays' Bez take part in this special edition for BBC Music Day. Along with their bandmates Candida Doyle and Rowetta Satchell, they join experts Catherine Southon and Mark Stacey scouring Kent Showground for items that will make them the most profit for charity. While they're busy, presenter Charlie Ross pays a visit to a recording studio in Wales, Rockfields

programme.title: Springwatch
programme.desc: Chris Packham and Michaela Strachan bring live updates from Norfolk, and Gillian Burke investigates some clever cuttlefish in Northern Ireland. Meanwhile, Iolo Williams tracks down mountain hares in the Highlands, and sea slugs making a colourful appearance

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest national and international stories as they break

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Tennis: Eastbourne
programme.desc: The Viking International Eastbourne. Clare Balding presents coverage of day four from Devonshire Park Lawn Tennis Club, featuring the quarter-finals. Ons Jabeur, Angelique Kerber, Kiki Bertens and Karolina Pliskova were the victorious players at this stage when the tournament was last held in 2019, defeating Alize Cornet, Simona Halep, Aryna Sabalenka and Ekaterina Alexandrova respectively. With commentary and analysis from Annabel Croft, Anne Keothavong, Sam Smith and Gigi Salmon

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: The team visits Manchester, where Caroline Hawley inspects an autograph from a rock 'n' roll legend, and Anita Manning values a concertina. Presenter Paul Martin learns about the city's musical heritage, from the Halle Orchestra to New Order and Oasis

programme.title: Live T20 Cricket
programme.desc: England v Sri Lanka. Isa Guha presents coverage of the second game in the three-match series, which comes from Sophia Gardens in Cardiff. Prior to the start of this series, the last meeting between the sides in this format was in Colombo in October 2018, when opener Jason Roy's impressive 69 off 36 balls and Joe Denly's bowling figures of 4-19 helped England to a 30-run victory. They will be hopeful of another strong performance this evening on home soil given they are currently at the top of the ICC Twenty20 rankings, with the Sri Lankans down in eighth place. With commentary from Michael Vaughan, Phil Tufnell, Jimmy Anderson, Ebony Rainford-Brent and Andy Zaltzman

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: A selection of highlights from the stand-up show focusing on a different topic each week. First up are John Bishop, Sarah Millican, Phil Wang, Ellie Taylor, Ed Byrne, Angela Barnes, Gary Delaney, Tom Stade and Laura Lexx giving their thoughts on relationships

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: The day's important national and international news stories. With Katie Razzall

programme.title: The Edge (2019)
programme.desc: Documentary about England's Test cricket team between 2009 and 2013, the first and only English side to ever reach the top of the world rankings. Featuring contributions from Andrew Strauss, Kevin Pietersen, Alastair Cook, James Anderson, Stuart Broad, Monty Panesar, Graeme Swann, Jonathan Trott, Matt Prior, Ian Bell, Paul Collingwood, Steven Finn, Tim Bresnan, David Saker and Andy Flower

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: Joe Lycett hosts the semi-final, as the four remaining sewers go back in time to the 1940s. Attempting to revive both the glamorous and thrifty styles of the decade, they tackle the decade's most elegant and innovative garments. First up, judges Patrick Grant and Esme Young challenge the sewers to make the decade's most famous trousers, Oxford bags. For the Transformation Challenge, the sewers are set a wartime task to turn old parachutes into glamorous dresses. In the Made to Measure challenge, the sewers take on their final icon of 40s fashion, Dior's New Look. Characterised by an hourglass silhouette and involving masses of fabric, the sewers must exaggerate their model's figure to create the iconic shape

programme.title: The Sheriffs Are Coming
programme.desc: Enforcement officers Ben and Myles turn detective to track down the assets owned by a tricky debtor whose car dealership owes thousands of pounds to an unhappy customer. Luke and Grant have an unusual job to do at a pizza shop when they find an unexpected squatter inside

programme.title: Dom Digs In
programme.desc: Dom Littlewood joins workers at the Port of Tyne, helping to unload cargo containers from the quayside, and discovering how their contents are transported around the country

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Over three rounds, contestants Candice, Esther, Craig and Shaun vie to find the single 'unbeatable' answer and streak ahead of the rest to make the final where they can win a cash prize. General knowledge quiz, hosted by Jason Manford

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Eric Knowles is joined by experts Caroline Hawley and Raj Bisram as two teams visit the Royal Welsh Showground in Builth Wells, scouring an antiques fair to pick up bargains. Eric also takes a little time out to travel across the border to Cirencester, Gloucestershire, to find some Roman treasures.

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: Deborah Meaden, Touker Suleyman, Tej Lalvani and Sara Davies are joined by former Dragon Theo Paphitis, who standing in for a self-isolating Peter Jones. The Dragons are served a selection of premium spiced rums by a confident drinks entrepreneur. A nursing bra designed to help mothers and babies breastfeed effectively and a heatless hair curling device invented in a garden shed are also offered up, while a Midlands-based duo pitch a voice-enhanced app for learner-readers. Presented by Evan Davis

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest national and international stories as they break

programme.title: Tennis: Eastbourne
programme.desc: The Viking International Eastbourne. Clare Balding presents coverage of day five from Devonshire Park Lawn Tennis Club, featuring the semi-finals. Little tennis was played at this stage when the tournament was last held in 2019, as Angelique Kerber received a walkover against the injured Ons Jabeur, before Karolina Pliskova produced a dominant display to prevail 6-1, 6-2 against Kiki Bertens

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Presenter Paul Martin and antiques experts Adam Partridge and Philip Serrell visit Ickworth House, a classical mansion near Bury St Edmunds in Suffolk. They pick out a selection of interesting collectibles to be sold at auction, including an East India Company medal and a vintage bathing costume. Paul also visits the Tate Britain in London to study the paintings of John Constable

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: In Stockholm, Michael Portillo braves a precarious tour of the city from its rooftops, before boarding a heritage tram to get the lowdown on 1930s Sweden from an expert

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: The day's important national and international news stories. With Faisal Islam

programme.title: Inside Culture
programme.desc: Mary Beard hands over the reins to cultural historian Shahidha Bari to explore the importance of self-expression, from the clothes we wear to the art and music we make. Shahidha visits artist Tracey Emin in her home town of Margate, and asks if is there a cost to revealing so much in your art. In Dundee, Shahidha also visits the new V&A exhibition on nightclub design

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Adam Frost offers tips on laying turf, and planting succulents. He also adds perennial plants to his kitchen garden to grow as cut flowers. Plus, Joe Swift travels to Marwood Hill Gardens in Devon to explore plants that thrive in moist conditions

programme.title: Glastonbury in the 90s
programme.desc: The story of Glastonbury in the 1990s, arguably the festival's redefining decade. It was the era television cameras came, dance music conquered and the festival changed beyond all recognition. Told through archival material from the time and by those who were there, it's voiced by Skin from Skunk Anansie, who were the last headliners of the decade

programme.title: Radiohead at Glastonbury 1997
programme.desc: The alternative rock band's set on the Pyramid Stage at the Somerset music festival, their first as headliners. The performance came two weeks after the release of the band's third album OK Computer, and songs included Paranoid Android, Karma Police, No Surprises and The Bends

programme.title: REM at Glastonbury 1999
programme.desc: The American band's performance on the Pyramid Stage, featuring performances of Daysleeper, The One I Love, Losing My Religion, Everybody Hurts and Man on the Moon

programme.title: VS. (2018)
programme.desc: Adam, a young man embittered by his experience in foster care, enters the hostile and competitive world of rap 'battling' when he is moved back to his home town of Southend. Drama, starring Connor Swindells and Fola Evans-Akingbola

programme.title: Between the Covers
programme.desc: Sara Cox is joined by Nish Kumar, Stacey Dooley, Caitlin Moran and Robert Webb to discuss Hamnet by Maggie O'Farrell, and The Last House on Needless Street by Catriona Ward, and they also share their own favourite books of all time

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Wild & Weird
programme.desc: Naomi Wilkinson and Tim Warwood investigate gruesome goings on, including a strange slime invading people's gardens, and a plant that pretends to be a dead animal

programme.title: The Dengineers
programme.desc: Rhodri, 10, is mad about cricket but is unable to go to his local club as often as he would like, so needs a cricket den where he can practice whenever he likes

programme.title: The Dog Ate My Homework
programme.desc: Team captains Orson and Janae battle it with the help of comedians Thanyia Moore and Helen Bauer, Officially Amazing's Ben Shires and Dr Ranj Singh

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: Richie and Mwaksy take on an army challenge

programme.title: What's Cooking Omari?
programme.desc: Brothers Omari and Laquarn make a banana shake breakfast shaky shake shake. This delicious breakfast is the perfect start for a busy day

programme.title: Deadly Dinosaurs with Steve Backshall
programme.desc: Steve is on a mission to find the weirdest dinosaur ever, starting with gigantoraptor, an eight-metre-tall giant that laid the biggest eggs in history

programme.title: Human Universe
programme.desc: On a trip to the fortified Moroccan village of Ait-Ben-Haddou in the Atlas Mountains, Professor Brian Cox reveals how by watching the stars' motion across the night sky, it is quite natural for man to think he is at the centre of everything. That view was held for many ages, but innate human curiosity has eventually led to an understanding of mankind's true place in space and time, and an appreciation that Earth is not a focal point but a mere particle of rock in a possibly infinite expanse of space, 13.8 billion years from the beginning of the universe

programme.title: The Outlaw (1943)
programme.desc: Notorious gunslinger Doc Holliday is reunited with his lawman friend Pat Garrett as he goes in search of his stolen horse. When he finds the thief is the infamous outlaw Billy the Kid, tensions grow between the three men, and their relationships with the same woman only make things worse. Howard Hughes' Western, starring Jane Russell, Walter Huston, Jack Buetel and Thomas Mitchell

programme.title: Mary Berry's Country House Secrets
programme.desc: Mary visits Powderham Castle in Devon to spend time with the Earl and Countess of Devon and their two young children, who are reinventing the Earldom for the 21st century. Mary's time with the family reveals stories of romance and tragedy as she discovers more of this extraordinary home. In the castle's historic kitchen, Mary prepares a Devon cream tea, before making a delicate peach posset for a Midsummer Eve woodland party the family are throwing to celebrate their two year anniversary at the castle

programme.title: Life in the Air
programme.desc: Wildlife series following animals that are able to defy gravity in various ways. The first programme features caracal cats, which jump to catch birds out of the air, and reveals how kangaroos' hopping movements help them find water in the desert. Leaping snakes are able to glide without wings in the jungles of Borneo while insects are able to accelerate up to 700G

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: For animals growing up is a real adventure, but finding food, avoiding predators and making friends doesn't always come naturally, so they need a little parental guidance to help them learn and hone the skills that will enable them to survive on their own. David Tennant narrates this documentary following the trials and tribulations of young animals all over the world as they prepare to leave home

programme.title: Brian Cox's Adventures in Space and Time
programme.desc: Brian explores the enigma of time, a phenomenon that is taken for granted but which, as far as physicists are concerned, is one of the biggest mysteries in the universe. He recalls highlights from his TV series that touch upon different sides of this physics conundrum, from breaking the sound barrier in a Eurofighter in order to see two sunsets in one day, to witnessing one of the oldest animal life cycles on Earth on a remote beach in Costa Rica

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: People are invited to try to make money out of their antiques by taking them to auction

programme.title: Casablanca (1942)
programme.desc: The American expat owner of a Moroccan nightclub is reunited with his old flame during the Second World War. Now married to a Czech freedom fighter, she needs his help to get them safely out of the country before they are captured by the Nazis - but the former lovers also have to wrestle the temptation to rekindle their simmering romance. Drama, with Humphrey Bogart, Ingrid Bergman, Claude Rains and Paul Henreid

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels from Stockholm into the Arctic Circle, along the way decorating a Dala horse and learning how Sweden created a welfare state

programme.title: T20 Cricket
programme.desc: England v Sri Lanka. Action from the third and final T20 of the series, taking place at Ageas Bowl in Southampton. The sides last met here in this format in July 2016, when Jos Buttler's innings of 73 not out helped England to an eight-wicket triumph

programme.title: Eric and Ernie - Behind the Scenes
programme.desc: Victoria Wood narrates the comedy duo's early years, considering why they remain Britain's best-loved double act. Archive footage and celebrity interviews combine to reveal the hard work that went into Morecambe and Wise's act and the secrets of their phenomenal success. Contributors include scriptwriter Eddie Braben, their widows Doreen and Joan, Cilla Black, Michael Grade, Miranda Hart, Penelope Keith and Andrew Marr

programme.title: Glastonbury in the 21st Century
programme.desc: Dizzee Rascal narrates a look at the festival since the turn of the century, with appearances by emerging global headliners such as Coldplay and the Killers, and a wildly packed Chemical Brothers performance from 2000. Plus, the much-debated arrival of hip-hop with Jay-Z in 2008, Beyoncé's game-changing performance in 2011, Kylie Minogue's return and Stormzy's defining headline set in 2019, as well as the Sunday afternoon Legend slots

programme.title: Glastonbury 2019
programme.desc: Lauren Laverne and Clara Amfo introduce Kylie Minogue's performance on the Pyramid Stage. This set marked the singer's first appearance at the festival, after being forced to pull out for health reasons in 2005, and featured hits from throughout her 30-year career. Plus, highlights from Miley Cyrus and Koffee's performances, and a film introduced by the Rev Kate Bottley exploring the spiritual history of Glastonbury

programme.title: Arctic Monkeys at Glastonbury 2013
programme.desc: The band's headline set on the Pyramid stage on the Friday night of the festival, featuring performances of songs including Do I Wanna Know?, I Bet You Look Good on the Dancefloor, Fake Tales of San Francisco and When the Sun Goes Down

programme.title: Captain Fantastic (2016)
programme.desc: A father raises his six children off the grid in the forests of the Pacific Northwest, creating a rigorous physical and intellectual process of education for them. He faces the challenge of reintegrating his family into society, a task that forces him to reassess his idea of what it means to be a parent. Comedy drama, starring Viggo Mortensen and Frank Langella

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross demonstrates how to paint a cold, dark winter night and a cosy little cabin on top of a snow-covered hill

programme.title: The Cruise
programme.desc: The ship sails into the western Caribbean. Mrs Goodman, angry about being on the wrong cruise, demands compensation. Scotty drags Jane up a waterfall in Jamaica. When they return to the ship, Jane is given her first audience ratings, and Scotty receives some shattering news

programme.title: Timeshift
programme.desc: Andrew Martin charts the evolution of British postage stamps and looks at how they became a national obsession, examining iconic examples and famous collectors, as well as meeting modern-day philatelists at a club

programme.title: Imagine
programme.desc: Alan Yentob travels to Hollywood to interview Mel Brooks, the comedy actor, writer, producer and director who turned 91 last year. At the time of filming, the musical theatre version of Brooks' 1974 comedy horror Young Frankenstein had just opened on London's West End. Yentob marks the occasion by profiling its creator, observing him at work and play, and revisiting archive interviews stretching back decades

programme.title: Blazing Saddles (1974)
programme.desc: A crooked railway tycoon whose men are terrorising a frontier town arranges for a black sheriff to be appointed, hoping the racist residents will reject him. The lawman proves more cunning than expected, and with the help of a drunken gunslinger, runs rings around the businessman. Mel Brooks' Western spoof, in which he co-stars alongside Cleavon Little, Gene Wilder and Madeline Kahn

programme.title: Philly DA: Breaking the Law
programme.desc: Documentary following veteran civil rights lawyer-turned-district attorney Larry Krasner as he adopts a radical approach to criminal justice in Philadelphia

programme.title: Philly DA: Breaking the Law
programme.desc: Newly elected DA Larry Krasner and his team begin a radical overhaul of Philadelphia's criminal justice system from within by poring over a cache of police misconduct archives

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross demonstrates how to paint a cold, dark winter night and a cosy little cabin on top of a snow-covered hill

programme.title: The Cruise
programme.desc: The ship sails into the western Caribbean. Mrs Goodman, angry about being on the wrong cruise, demands compensation. Scotty drags Jane up a waterfall in Jamaica. When they return to the ship, Jane is given her first audience ratings, and Scotty receives some shattering news

programme.title: Timeshift
programme.desc: Andrew Martin charts the evolution of British postage stamps and looks at how they became a national obsession, examining iconic examples and famous collectors, as well as meeting modern-day philatelists at a club

programme.title: Singer-Songwriters at the BBC
programme.desc: A compilation of performances highlighting the growth of the singer-songwriter genre in the 1970s. Featuring songs by Bobbie Gentry, Kris Kristofferson, Buffy Sainte-Marie, Janis Ian, Gordon Lightfoot, John Martyn, Randy Newman, Joni Mitchell, Don McLean, Linda Lewis, Ralph McTell, Paul Brady and Loudon Wainwright III

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Simon Mayo and Lenny Henry present the edition first aired on March 14 1991, featuring performances by Chesney Hawkes, REM, Ride, the Bee Gees, Queen, Massive Attack, Rod Stewart, Happy Mondays, the Clash and Jesus Jones

programme.title: Top of the Pops

programme.title: Al Green at Glastonbury 1999

programme.title: Best of Glastonbury 1998
programme.desc: Highlights of the year's summer festival, one of the muddiest on record. programme.title: Best of Glastonbury 1999
programme.desc: Highlights of the massive summer festival which saw thousands of music fans swarming to the legendary site to view acts including REM, the Beautiful South, Blondie, Barenaked Ladies, Björn Again, Manic Street Preachers, Underworld, Texas, Beth Orton, Eliza Carthy, Billy Bragg, Skunk Anansie, Fun Lovin' Criminals, Lenny Kravitz, the Corrs and Al Green

programme.title: A Year in the Wild
programme.desc: A tour of Britain's largest national park, a land of extremes in the heart of the Highlands, where a diverse range of wildlife including golden eagles, ptarmigan, capercaillie, red deer and crested tits can be found. The programme explores the area through the eyes of people who live and work there, including a mountain guide, an artist, a gamekeeper and a nature writer. Narrated by Hermione Norris

programme.title: Michael Palin's New Europe
programme.desc: The veteran traveller completes his journey around eastern Europe. He learns how to skin a pig and make sausages in the Tatra mountains of Slovakia, enjoys a mud bath with the 2006 Miss World Tatana Kucharova in the spa town of Karlovy Vary and visits Turba's famous mime school in the Czech Republic. Finally, he travels to the holiday complex at Prora on the island of Rug, Germany, which was built by Hitler for his 'Strength Through Joy' programme

programme.title: Best of Glastonbury 2003
programme.desc: Highlights of the 2003 three-day festival, which featured performances by the Manic Street Preachers, REM, Radiohead, Macy Gray, Sugababes, Feeder, Echo and the Bunnymen and Fatboy Slim

programme.title: Iggy Pop and the Stooges at Glastonbury 2007

programme.title: Glastonbury 2017
programme.desc: Jo Whiley and Mark Radcliffe introduce highlights from the festival at Worthy Farm in Somerset, where Radiohead, Foo Fighters and Ed Sheeran were the headliners, including a reminder of some of the big performances, stories and moments from the weekend's events.

Toodloo helps the line get a little longer still but when Great BigHoo taps the screen, the line becomes so long it shoots off the Twirlywoo Screen. programme.desc: Love Monster visits Dr Piggles on Ha Ha Ha Day, only to find that his friend has the hiccups and is unable to make his patients laugh. Dr Piggles asks Love Monster if he can fill in for him while he rests, assuring him that he'll be right there to help out. But when the hiccups get worse, he has to lock himself in the back room, and Love Monster must find ways to make all the patients laugh by himself.

programme.title: B.O.T and the Beasties
programme.desc: The Snowy Blower lives underground and loves blowing fountains of snow up into the air! B.O.T. must keep clear of the snowy eruptions and be quick to complete this mission.

programme.title: Something Special
programme.desc: Justin and his friends fly a hawk, and Mr Tumble takes a flight on an aeroplane

programme.title: Maddie, the Zoo and You
programme.desc: Maddie discovers how different animals move in different ways and learns about grip.

programme.title: Timmy Time
programme.desc: Osbourne shows Timmy how to clean his tricycle after a muddy ride around the playground

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: Honey’s trip to see Dr. Bingo keeps getting interrupted by other, more exciting patients.

programme.title: Messy Goes to OKIDO
programme.desc: Messy, Felix, Zoe and Zoom go in search of a light that never goes out

programme.title: Apple Tree House
programme.desc: Zainab shows us that stars and landmarks can help us navigate our environment

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: Evie, Doug, Jess and Gramps take Waffle to his first puppy training class

programme.title: Andy's Aquatic Adventures
programme.desc: Mr Hammond has been taking photos with his new camera for an underwater sea-creatures exhibit happening at Safari World. Jen has been downloading them in the Aqua Base

programme.title: Garden Tales
programme.desc: Freya the Frog has just taken her first leap out of the tropical pond. She’s leaving all the tadpoles behind to explore the big wide world. She’s ready for anything, but her confidence gets her into a sticky situation. After all, she is a very tiny frog in a very big garden. Luckily, Freya meets Sid the Snail and Wilbur the Worm on the way, and they help her on her journey.

programme.title: Our Family
programme.desc: Mum and Dad have a treat in store for George and Freddie. It’s a special tennis game including dinosaurs, bees, and smiley faces. But will they score an ace? Later, George and Freddie get the face paints out and transform Mum and Dad into colourful cats. What will they think of their amazing make overs?

programme.title: The Baby Club
programme.desc: Giovanna and Baby Bear welcome babies Alara, Samaa, Reece, Eleanor, Ty’lana and their grown-ups to The Baby Club. Parents, carers and their little ones at home are invited to join in with all the fun activities too! Inside Giovanna’s bag is are some scented lavender bags, and all the parents and babies have different objects and toys that are smelly. These are explored through discovery, play, song and story. Grown-ups at home are encouraged to take part by hiding safe, baby-appropriate smelly objects and toys inside a fabric bag or pillowcase for them to reveal and explore with their baby! The children’s nursery rhyme Ring A Ring O' Roses features, as does a story involving Baby Bear, and viewers at home show how they’ve been exploring smelly things too. The Action Song gets everyone moving about before some calm time with sensory lights and the Goodbye Song.

programme.title: Magic Hands
programme.desc: Aimee and Ashley set sail to ancient Greece and Bobbles has great fun as the one-eyed giant Cyclops. Deep in a dark cave Leapy Lion dressed as Odysseus is hiding from Cyclops. With the help of his animal friends Odysseus and his sailors put on a clever disguise to flee from the monster to their waiting ship

programme.title: What's On Your Head?
programme.desc: Firefighter Karen shows us her fabulous helmet. We see how important it is for Karen to wear it while she does her heroic job, as well as some of the amazing things it does.

programme.title: CBeebies Goldilocks & the Three Bears
programme.desc: Join Mr Bloom and the Tiddlers as they accept an invitation to tea. Goldilocks and her Mum are waiting for their guests

programme.title: Sarah & Duck
programme.desc: The Ribbon sisters invite Sarah and her feathered friend to the park for their favourite seasonal event

programme.title: Charlie and Lola
programme.desc: The duo have a race around the park, and Lola realises it is not necessary to win all the time

programme.title: Tee and Mo
programme.desc: The animated adventures of an energetic baby monkey and his long-suffering mother, who take a light-hearted approach to problem-solving and teamwork as they tackle life's challenges together

programme.title: Clangers
programme.desc: Granny, Tiny and Small find a forgotten tunnel leading somewhere unexpected

programme.title: In the Night Garden
programme.desc: Makka Pakka shares out his six stones, but the Pontipines steal his last one. Narrated by Derek Jacobi

programme.title: CBeebies Bedtime Stories
programme.desc: Tom Fletcher reads Daddy's Sandwich, by Pip Jones. A little girl goes on a quest to make her daddy the perfect sandwich, filled with all of his favourite things

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts and the team explore a large house in Penge, London, and two terraces in Silverdale, Staffordshire, and Gunhill, Coventry, before they are sold at auction

programme.title: Rip Off Britain: Holidays
programme.desc: Angela Rippon, Gloria Hunniford and Julia Somerville focus on the devastating impact of coronavirus on travel, including the insurance company that refused to pay out after a couple's grand tour of South America came to a grinding halt. They also speak to a customer who was forced to turn to local police for help after her online travel agency failed to answer its 24-hour emergency helpline

programme.title: The Moment of Proof
programme.desc: How a simple business card revealed the true identities of a gang of con artists, and a ripped-up note that held the key to locking away two murderers

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Eric Knowles is joined by experts Caroline Hawley and Raj Bisram as two teams visit the Royal Welsh Showground in Builth Wells, scouring an antiques fair to pick up bargains. Eric also takes a little time out to travel across the border to Cirencester, Gloucestershire, to find some Roman treasures.

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: While Daniel and Bear's concern grows for Sid, Jimmi is delighted to welcome a travelling friend who has come to spread some joy. Local bad boys Aaron and Lenny have big plans for the day, but don't anticipate meeting Rob and Harriet who are determined to stop them

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Over three rounds, contestants Candice, Esther, Craig and Shaun vie to find the single 'unbeatable' answer and streak ahead of the rest to make the final where they can win a cash prize. General knowledge quiz, hosted by Jason Manford

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin helps a rural property-shopping couple with an £850,000 budget put down roots closer to family in Somerset. Along the way, Jonnie visits Glastonbury and learns how its religious past and strong links to English legend still resonate with people there today

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Harry and Anna want the garden of the house they share in Romsey, Hampshire, to be less about raised beds and more about raised cocktail glasses. However, their garden is currently a boring, blank canvas. To realise their dreams of a stylish social garden, the duo call upon the services of Charlie Dimmock and the David and Harry Rich to perform the transformation they need on a budget of £4,000

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Dealers Ian Humphries, Jane Cave, Adrian Higham, James Gooch, Natasha Francis and Tracy Martin go head to head over a polychrome carving of St Sebastian, a pair of vintage Bakelite phones, some Chinese fortune sticks and a piece of rock 'n' roll history. Presented by Nigel Havers

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Ronan Keating host the live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: The discovery of a double crime prompts Sonia to point the finger of suspicion at Rocky. Bobby decides to come clean to Dana, while Ruby and Martin receive another setback. Suki is furious with Kheerat, so he tells Sharon it's over for the sake of his family. But Suki's anger turns to upset when she hears some shocking news

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Chelsea lands herself in hot water with the police, and when Jack suggests she gets herself a lawyer, she asks Gray out for lunch - and it isn't long before the two are flirting. Isaac's obsession continues to build, Ben opens up to Callum and Peter encourages Bobby to fight for Dana

programme.title: Tudur's TV Flashback
programme.desc: Tudur Owen reviews TV clips themed on the seaside, from a day trip to a world of death-defying stunts and man-eating sharks to woollen bikinis

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: Theo Paphitis steps in for Peter Jones, joining regular Dragons Deborah Meaden, Touker Suleyman, Tej Lalvani and Sara Davies to assess more money-making schemes. A musical inventor shows off his drumming training device, and four children make a first aid demonstration to secure investment for their parents' business. Plus, pitches for culinary alcohol and a prototype shoe mechanism

programme.desc: Fiona Bruce introduces the topical debate, with a virtual audience from around the UK. programme.title: Newscast
programme.desc: Adam Fleming, Laura Kuenssberg and Chris Mason present their weekly round-up from Westminster, delivering their usual mix of serious analysis and light-hearted gossip about the biggest stories in politics

programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell follow the development progress of auction-bought properties in Baddeley Green in Stoke, Rushden in Northampton and the village of Cwm near Ebbw Vale in Blaenau Gwent

programme.title: Rip Off Britain: Holidays
programme.desc: The travellers still fighting for refunds months after their holidays were cancelled, and a couple asked to pay out for a transatlantic flight to a cruise that never took place. Plus, the story of the customers given false hope after an insolvent coach holiday company was brought back to life by new owners. Presented by Angela Rippon, Gloria Hunniford and Julia Somerville

programme.title: The Moment of Proof
programme.desc: How a discarded gun cartridge assisted police in convicting a man who had gunned down a fellow officer. Plus, the packet of medication that helped lock up a gang of violent jewellery thieves. Narrated by Chuku Modu

programme.title: BBC Wales Today Coronavirus Update
programme.desc: The latest developments, including the daily Welsh Government news conference

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Over three rounds, contestants John, Maria, Caroline and Mark vie to find the single 'unbeatable' answer and streak ahead of the rest to make the final where they can win a cash prize. General knowledge quiz, hosted by Jason Manford

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross looks at buying and selling design classics of the past, looking back at some of the best buys from past programmes. As well as revisiting some of the show's favourite experts and auctioneers, Charlie recalls a visit to the Brighton Pavilion where he learned all about its connection with a famous hat designer

programme.title: Home Is Where the Art Is
programme.desc: Nick Knowles challenges three artists to create a suitable piece to celebrate the upcoming wedding of paraclimbing champion Richard and fiancee Keely. Can a metal sculptor, a stained-glass designer or a paint and textile artist create just what the pair are looking for?

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: In Malvern, Worcestershire, Miriam and Christopher have a concrete garden, but want one inspired by their time in Indonesia. Charlie Dimmock and brothers Harry and David Rich come up with plans to transform the space on a budget of £3,000

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: The dealers are offered to chance to bid on a mid 20th-century French cabinet used to display a bride's veil, an unusual wooden children's toy and a round metal case for holding needles and shuttles. Presented by Nigel Havers

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Ronan Keating present the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Iqra offers advice to Bobby about lying in relationships, only to make her feel bad about being deceitful herself - so she decides to reveal the truth to Mila. Sheree is shocked by Chelsea's plan to wrap Gray around her little finger so she doesn't have to pay for his legal services. Vinny hits rock bottom and Rainie tells Bernie she is still not sure who she will choose as her surrogate

programme.title: Ant-Man (2015)
programme.desc: A thief attempting to go straight is recruited by a scientist who provides him with a suit that gives him the power to shrink. He must use his new skills to carry out a special mission - helping the inventor to steal back his miniaturisation technology from a ruthless businessman who intends to turn it into a deadly weapon. Superhero adventure, starring Paul Rudd, Michael Douglas and Evangeline Lilly

programme.title: The Producers (1968)
programme.desc: A crooked Broadway producer and his neurotic accountant discover they can make a fortune by putting on a play that closes on opening night, then pocketing the unspent budget. The duo set out to put on the worst show of all time - a tasteless little number called Springtime for Hitler written by a deranged former Nazi. Mel Brooks' comedy, starring Zero Mostel, Gene Wilder and Kenneth Mars

programme.title: Best of Top Gear
programme.desc: More highlights from recent series of the motoring show. Featuring another outing for Paddy McGuinness's ice cream van, a tribute to Stirling Moss, the Top Gear Mid Life Crisis Triathlon, and two very different takes on Italian Supercar

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Sabrina Ghayour and Shivi Ramoutar and special guest Jack Savoretti. Olly Smith joins via video link to pick wines to go with the studio dishes

programme.title: Our Food, Our Family with Michela Chiappa
programme.desc: The Welsh-Italian cook meets four generations of food lovers in Cardiff. While joining the Aslams to rustle up some of their favourite curries, fish dishes and a keema bolognese, Michela meets 90-year-old head of the family Mohammed, who came from Bangladesh in 1958 and went on to open one of Wales' very first Asian restaurants. Michela tries to prise the family's secret spice mix as well as helping grandson Yusuf to master one of his grandmother's specialities to bring along to a special party to celebrate his grandparents' 54th wedding anniversary

programme.title: Tennis: Eastbourne
programme.desc: The Viking International Eastbourne. Clare Balding presents coverage of the women's singles final on day five from Devonshire Park Lawn Tennis Club. Karolina Pliskova of the Czech Republic took the honours when this tournament was last staged in 2019, prevailing 6-1, 6-4 against Germany's 2018 Wimbledon champion Angelique Kerber in the showpiece match

programme.title: Pizza Boys
programme.desc: Following Jez and Ieuan, two young men from Llanelli who had a dream to change their lives and become pizza-makers extraordinaire. The pair decided to buy an old Italian van, put an oven in the back and set off on an adventure to enter the World Pizza Championships in Parma. In the first episode, the pair get some last minute tips from their favourite Cardiff pizzeria, host their own special send-off before packing up their van, Smokey Pete, and hitting the road

programme.title: Tudur's TV Flashback
programme.desc: Comedian Tudur Owen is on a quest for adventure as he picks through another selection of funny and fascinating clips from the past 50 years of Welsh TV, featuring everything from bucking broncos and medieval warriors to Hawaiian hula girls

programme.title: MOTD Live: UEFA Euro 2020
programme.desc: Wales v Denmark (Kick-off 5.00pm). Coverage of the opening match in the last 16, which takes place at Johan Cruijff ArenA in Amsterdam. Wales only won one of their group matches, but that victory against Turkey and their draw against Switzerland was enough to see them finish second behind Italy, who romped through the group stage, winning all three matches without conceding a goal. Denmark seemed to be heading for an early exit after losing their opening two fixtures against Finland and Belgium, but a remarkable 4-1 thumping of Russia, and other results going their way, saw them progress as Group B runners-up

programme.title: MOTD Live: UEFA Euro 2020
programme.desc: Italy v Austria (Kick-off 8.00pm). All the action from the second last-16 fixture, which takes place at Wembley Stadium. The Italians have been one of the outstanding teams in the tournament so far, moving from dark horses to being one of the favourites to lift the trophy. They defeated both Turkey and Switzerland 3-0, before dominating Wales in a 1-0 victory to stretch their winning record to 10 consecutive matches, all of which have been without conceding a single goal. Austria finished second behind the Netherlands in Group C, defeating North Macedonia and Ukraine to progress beyond the opening group phase of a major tournament for the first time since 1982

programme.title: Elis James' Feast of Football
programme.desc: Elis James is joined by guests Danny Gabbidon and Jayne Ludlow, for all the reaction to Wales' last 16 match against Denmark

programme.title: UEFA Euro 2020 Highlights
programme.desc: Wales v Denmark and Italy v Austria. The opening two last-16 matches, held at Johan Cruijff ArenA in Amsterdam and Wembley Stadium, as the first two quarter-finalists were decided

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Comedy panel show, hosted by Rob Brydon, in which team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack are joined by celebrity guests to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves

programme.title: MOTD: UEFA Euro 2020 Replay
programme.desc: A chance to see one of the opening two last-16 matches, from either Johan Cruijff ArenA in Amsterdam or Wembley Stadium
