With an ugly concrete outhouse, dangerous broken steps, mismatched levels and wild overgrown corners, the current garden is neither safe not attractive

programme.title: MOTD Live: UEFA Euro 2020
programme.desc: Wales v Denmark (Kick-off 5.00pm). Coverage of the opening match in the last 16, which takes place at Johan Cruijff ArenA in Amsterdam. Wales only won one of their group matches, but that victory against Turkey and their draw against Switzerland was enough to see them finish second behind Italy, who romped through the group stage, winning all three matches without conceding a goal. Denmark seemed to be heading for an early exit after losing their opening two fixtures against Finland and Belgium, but a remarkable 4-1 thumping of Russia, and other results going their way, saw them progress as Group B runners-up

programme.title: MOTD Live: UEFA Euro 2020
programme.desc: Italy v Austria (Kick-off 8.00pm). All the action from the second last-16 fixture, which takes place at Wembley Stadium. The Italians have been one of the outstanding teams in the tournament so far, moving from dark horses to being one of the favourites to lift the trophy. They defeated both Turkey and Switzerland 3-0, before dominating Wales in a 1-0 victory to stretch their winning record to 10 consecutive matches, all of which have been without conceding a single goal. Austria finished second behind the Netherlands in Group C, defeating North Macedonia and Ukraine to progress beyond the opening group phase of a major tournament for the first time since 1982

programme.title: UEFA Euro 2020 Highlights
programme.desc: Wales v Denmark and Italy v Austria. The opening two last-16 matches, held at Johan Cruijff ArenA in Amsterdam and Wembley Stadium, as the first two quarter-finalists were decided

programme.title: Best of Top Gear
programme.desc: Highlights from series 29 and 30, including the time when the presenters were challenged to spend 24 hours in company cars in Paddy McGuinness' home town of Bolton, and Freddie Flintoff racing an Extreme E electric off-roader against a man in a jet pack

programme.title: MOTD: UEFA Euro 2020 Replay
programme.desc: A chance to see one of the opening two last-16 matches, from either Johan Cruijff ArenA in Amsterdam or Wembley Stadium

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts and the team explore a large house in Penge, London, and two terraces in Silverdale, Staffordshire, and Gunhill, Coventry, before they are sold at auction

programme.title: Rip Off Britain: Holidays
programme.desc: Angela Rippon, Gloria Hunniford and Julia Somerville focus on the devastating impact of coronavirus on travel, including the insurance company that refused to pay out after a couple's grand tour of South America came to a grinding halt. They also speak to a customer who was forced to turn to local police for help after her online travel agency failed to answer its 24-hour emergency helpline

programme.title: The Moment of Proof
programme.desc: How a simple business card revealed the true identities of a gang of con artists, and a ripped-up note that held the key to locking away two murderers

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Eric Knowles is joined by experts Caroline Hawley and Raj Bisram as two teams visit the Royal Welsh Showground in Builth Wells, scouring an antiques fair to pick up bargains. Eric also takes a little time out to travel across the border to Cirencester, Gloucestershire, to find some Roman treasures.

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: While Daniel and Bear's concern grows for Sid, Jimmi is delighted to welcome a travelling friend who has come to spread some joy. Local bad boys Aaron and Lenny have big plans for the day, but don't anticipate meeting Rob and Harriet who are determined to stop them

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Over three rounds, contestants Candice, Esther, Craig and Shaun vie to find the single 'unbeatable' answer and streak ahead of the rest to make the final where they can win a cash prize. General knowledge quiz, hosted by Jason Manford

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin helps a rural property-shopping couple with an £850,000 budget put down roots closer to family in Somerset. Along the way, Jonnie visits Glastonbury and learns how its religious past and strong links to English legend still resonate with people there today

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Harry and Anna want the garden of the house they share in Romsey, Hampshire, to be less about raised beds and more about raised cocktail glasses. However, their garden is currently a boring, blank canvas. To realise their dreams of a stylish social garden, the duo call upon the services of Charlie Dimmock and the David and Harry Rich to perform the transformation they need on a budget of £4,000

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Dealers Ian Humphries, Jane Cave, Adrian Higham, James Gooch, Natasha Francis and Tracy Martin go head to head over a polychrome carving of St Sebastian, a pair of vintage Bakelite phones, some Chinese fortune sticks and a piece of rock 'n' roll history. Presented by Nigel Havers

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Ronan Keating host the live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: The discovery of a double crime prompts Sonia to point the finger of suspicion at Rocky. Bobby decides to come clean to Dana, while Ruby and Martin receive another setback. Suki is furious with Kheerat, so he tells Sharon it's over for the sake of his family. But Suki's anger turns to upset when she hears some shocking news

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Chelsea lands herself in hot water with the police, and when Jack suggests she gets herself a lawyer, she asks Gray out for lunch - and it isn't long before the two are flirting. Isaac's obsession continues to build, Ben opens up to Callum and Peter encourages Bobby to fight for Dana

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Jo Brand, Simon Day, Kiri Pritchard-McLean and Henning Wehn join host Rob Brydon and regular captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show. As usual, the participants set out to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible fibs about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory, through rounds including Home Truths and Quick-fire Lies

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: Theo Paphitis steps in for Peter Jones, joining regular Dragons Deborah Meaden, Touker Suleyman, Tej Lalvani and Sara Davies to assess more money-making schemes. A musical inventor shows off his drumming training device, and four children make a first aid demonstration to secure investment for their parents' business. Plus, pitches for culinary alcohol and a prototype shoe mechanism

programme.title: The View
programme.desc: Mark Carruthers presents a review of the week's political news, including reports on the latest events at Stormont and Westminster. Plus, comment and analysis with studio guests and an alternative view on the week's political headlines

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce introduces the topical debate, with a virtual audience from around the UK. This week's panellists are Secretary of State for Justice and Lord Chancellor Robert Buckland MP, Shadow Health and Social Care Secretary Jonathan Ashworth MP, Green Party MP Caroline Lucas, Chair of the NHS Confederation Victor Adebowale and Economics correspondent at the Spectator Kate Andrews

programme.title: Newscast
programme.desc: Adam Fleming, Laura Kuenssberg and Chris Mason present their weekly round-up from Westminster, delivering their usual mix of serious analysis and light-hearted gossip about the biggest stories in politics

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: Romero's newly stable, blissful life is placed in jeopardy by his latest investigation. Meanwhile, Norman considers holding back certain key facts from Dr Edwards. Thriller, starring Vera Farmiga and Freddie Highmore

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell follow the development progress of auction-bought properties in Baddeley Green in Stoke, Rushden in Northampton and the village of Cwm near Ebbw Vale in Blaenau Gwent

programme.title: Rip Off Britain: Holidays
programme.desc: The travellers still fighting for refunds months after their holidays were cancelled, and a couple asked to pay out for a transatlantic flight to a cruise that never took place. Plus, the story of the customers given false hope after an insolvent coach holiday company was brought back to life by new owners. Presented by Angela Rippon, Gloria Hunniford and Julia Somerville

programme.title: The Moment of Proof
programme.desc: How a discarded gun cartridge assisted police in convicting a man who had gunned down a fellow officer. Plus, the packet of medication that helped lock up a gang of violent jewellery thieves. Narrated by Chuku Modu

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross looks at buying and selling design classics of the past, looking back at some of the best buys from past programmes. As well as revisiting some of the show's favourite experts and auctioneers, Charlie recalls a visit to the Brighton Pavilion where he learned all about its connection with a famous hat designer

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Over three rounds, contestants John, Maria, Caroline and Mark vie to find the single 'unbeatable' answer and streak ahead of the rest to make the final where they can win a cash prize. General knowledge quiz, hosted by Jason Manford

programme.title: Home Is Where the Art Is
programme.desc: Nick Knowles challenges three artists to create a suitable piece to celebrate the upcoming wedding of paraclimbing champion Richard and fiancee Keely. Can a metal sculptor, a stained-glass designer or a paint and textile artist create just what the pair are looking for?

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin heads to the Wye Valley, where he helps an entrepreneurial couple with a £650,000 budget find a dream home which allows them to build a rural business. Along the way he visits the UK's first solar aquaponics greenhouse and discovers how 21st-century technology there could be the sustainable solution to growing enough food to solve the world's food shortage

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: In Malvern, Worcestershire, Miriam and Christopher have a concrete garden, but want one inspired by their time in Indonesia. Charlie Dimmock and brothers Harry and David Rich come up with plans to transform the space on a budget of £3,000

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: The dealers are offered to chance to bid on a mid 20th-century French cabinet used to display a bride's veil, an unusual wooden children's toy and a round metal case for holding needles and shuttles. Presented by Nigel Havers

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Ronan Keating present the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Iqra offers advice to Bobby about lying in relationships, only to make her feel bad about being deceitful herself - so she decides to reveal the truth to Mila. Sheree is shocked by Chelsea's plan to wrap Gray around her little finger so she doesn't have to pay for his legal services. Vinny hits rock bottom and Rainie tells Bernie she is still not sure who she will choose as her surrogate

programme.title: Ant-Man (2015)
programme.desc: A thief attempting to go straight is recruited by a scientist who provides him with a suit that gives him the power to shrink. He must use his new skills to carry out a special mission - helping the inventor to steal back his miniaturisation technology from a ruthless businessman who intends to turn it into a deadly weapon. Superhero adventure, starring Paul Rudd, Michael Douglas and Evangeline Lilly

programme.title: The Producers (1968)
programme.desc: A crooked Broadway producer and his neurotic accountant discover they can make a fortune by putting on a play that closes on opening night, then pocketing the unspent budget. The duo set out to put on the worst show of all time - a tasteless little number called Springtime for Hitler written by a deranged former Nazi. Mel Brooks' comedy, starring Zero Mostel, Gene Wilder and Kenneth Mars

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: Norman is forced to confront his inner demons as his sessions with Dr Edwards progress. Meanwhile, Norma tries to keep her own problems hidden

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Sabrina Ghayour and Shivi Ramoutar and special guest Jack Savoretti. Olly Smith joins via video link to pick wines to go with the studio dishes

programme.title: Our Food, Our Family with Michela Chiappa
programme.desc: The Welsh-Italian cook meets four generations of food lovers in Cardiff. While joining the Aslams to rustle up some of their favourite curries, fish dishes and a keema bolognese, Michela meets 90-year-old head of the family Mohammed, who came from Bangladesh in 1958 and went on to open one of Wales' very first Asian restaurants. Michela tries to prise the family's secret spice mix as well as helping grandson Yusuf to master one of his grandmother's specialities to bring along to a special party to celebrate his grandparents' 54th wedding anniversary

programme.title: Tennis: Eastbourne
programme.desc: The Viking International Eastbourne. Clare Balding presents coverage of the women's singles final on day five from Devonshire Park Lawn Tennis Club. Karolina Pliskova of the Czech Republic took the honours when this tournament was last staged in 2019, prevailing 6-1, 6-4 against Germany's 2018 Wimbledon champion Angelique Kerber in the showpiece match

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore sees what people are about to throw away at a Surrey recycling centre, rescuing two car axle stands, a farmhouse table and some rusty old railings for transformation

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Arit Anderson and Harry and David Rich compete as they design gardens for a Swansea couple who want a family space suitable for their two small children. With an ugly concrete outhouse, dangerous broken steps, mismatched levels and wild overgrown corners, the current garden is neither safe not attractive

programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as contestants Ryan, Faith, Jake and Libby pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser. programme.desc: Faith is determined to help Liv. Tracy struggles to contain her guilt. Dawn demands answers

In Malibu Hills, he meets one of the most famous dog families, that of Lassie, then travels to the Arabian desert to ride with Bedouin horseman Ali Al Ameri, who trains the world's oldest man-made horses - the Arabian - for TV shows. Martin then spends time with Josef Svoboda, a logging superstar from the Czech Republic who uses heavy horses in his work

programme.desc: The chef is joined by guest Kirsty Gallacher, and chefs Thomasina Miers and Francesco Mazzei, and heads off on a food adventure to Kent. This week's recipes include langoustines, buttermilk fried chicken, mussels and sole, and a masterclass in celeriac

programme.title: Simply Raymond Blanc
programme.desc: The chef reflects on how the British food scene has changed since he first came to the UK over 30 years ago. He whips up a pea soup, stuffed peppers, and his own take on brown sauce. He is joined by Paul Ainsworth, who prepares an alternative cheese board

programme.title: James Martin's American Adventure
programme.desc: The chef visits Austin in Texas, starting with a breakfast taco at the iconic Torchy's, followed by a cowboy-themed shopping spree. He then cooks in front of a mural dedicated to his favourite ingredient - butter

programme.title: ITV Racing Live
programme.desc: Oli Bell presents coverage of the 2.25 Chipchase Stakes and 3.30 Northumberland Plate from Newcastle, the 3.45 Irish Derby from The Curragh, and action from Newmarket. With analysis from Jason Weaver and Adele Mulrennan, reports by Rishi Persad, Matt Chapman and Mick Fitzgerald, and commentary from Mark Johnson

programme.title: UEFA Euro 2020 Live
programme.desc: Wales v Denmark (Kick-off 5.00pm). Coverage of the opening match in the last 16, which takes place at Johan Cruijff ArenA in Amsterdam. Wales only won one of their group matches, but that victory against Turkey and their draw against Switzerland was enough to see them finish second behind Italy, who romped through the group stage, winning all three matches without conceding a goal. Denmark seemed to be heading for an early exit after losing their opening two fixtures against Finland and Belgium, but a remarkable 4-1 thumping of Russia, and other results going their way, saw them progress as Group B runners-up

programme.title: UEFA Euro 2020 Live
programme.desc: Italy v Austria (Kick-off 8.00pm). All the action from the second last-16 fixture, which takes place at Wembley Stadium. The Italians have been one of the outstanding teams in the tournament so far, moving from dark horses to being one of the favourites to lift the trophy. They defeated both Turkey and Switzerland 3-0, before dominating Wales in a 1-0 victory to stretch their winning record to 10 consecutive matches, all of which have been without conceding a single goal. Austria finished second behind the Netherlands in Group C, defeating North Macedonia and Ukraine to progress beyond the opening group phase of a major tournament for the first time since 1982

programme.title: It'll Be Alright on the Night
programme.desc: David Walliams narrates a compilation of TV clangers from the past year's programmes. Featuring live on-air blunders from Stormzy, Robbie Williams, Craig Revel Horwood, Jonathan Ross, and Ant & Dec, as well as a series of outtakes starring Will.i.am and Jeremy Clarkson. Plus, two toddlers run rings around Kay Burley, and Vicky Pattison forgets the name of the show she's presenting

programme.title: Tour de France Highlights
programme.desc: Action from stage one of the most prestigious race on the calendar, featuring a 198km route from Brest to Landerneau. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as contestants Ryan, Faith, Jake and Libby pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser. In Malibu Hills, he meets one of the most famous dog families, that of Lassie, then travels to the Arabian desert to ride with Bedouin horseman Ali Al Ameri, who trains the world's oldest man-made horses - the Arabian - for TV shows. Martin then spends time with Josef Svoboda, a logging superstar from the Czech Republic who uses heavy horses in his work

programme.title: Diana

programme.desc: The chef is joined by guest Kirsty Gallacher, and chefs Thomasina Miers and Francesco Mazzei, and heads off on a food adventure to Kent. This week's recipes include langoustines, buttermilk fried chicken, mussels and sole, and a masterclass in celeriac

programme.title: Simply Raymond Blanc

programme.title: James Martin's American Adventure
programme.desc: The chef visits Austin in Texas, starting with a breakfast taco at the iconic Torchy's, followed by a cowboy-themed shopping spree. He then cooks in front of a mural dedicated to his favourite ingredient - butter

programme.title: ITV Racing Live
programme.desc: Oli Bell presents coverage of the 2.25 Chipchase Stakes and 3.30 Northumberland Plate from Newcastle, the 3.45 Irish Derby from The Curragh, and action from Newmarket. With analysis from Jason Weaver and Adele Mulrennan, reports by Rishi Persad, Matt Chapman and Mick Fitzgerald, and commentary from Mark Johnson

programme.title: UEFA Euro 2020 Live
programme.desc: Wales v Denmark (Kick-off 5.00pm). Coverage of the opening match in the last 16, which takes place at Johan Cruijff ArenA in Amsterdam. Wales only won one of their group matches, but that victory against Turkey and their draw against Switzerland was enough to see them finish second behind Italy, who romped through the group stage, winning all three matches without conceding a goal. Denmark seemed to be heading for an early exit after losing their opening two fixtures against Finland and Belgium, but a remarkable 4-1 thumping of Russia, and other results going their way, saw them progress as Group B runners-up

programme.title: UEFA Euro 2020 Live
programme.desc: Italy v Austria (Kick-off 8.00pm). All the action from the second last-16 fixture, which takes place at Wembley Stadium. The Italians have been one of the outstanding teams in the tournament so far, moving from dark horses to being one of the favourites to lift the trophy. They defeated both Turkey and Switzerland 3-0, before dominating Wales in a 1-0 victory to stretch their winning record to 10 consecutive matches, all of which have been without conceding a single goal. Austria finished second behind the Netherlands in Group C, defeating North Macedonia and Ukraine to progress beyond the opening group phase of a major tournament for the first time since 1982

programme.title: It'll Be Alright on the Night
programme.desc: David Walliams narrates a compilation of TV clangers from the past year's programmes. Featuring live on-air blunders from Stormzy, Robbie Williams, Craig Revel Horwood, Jonathan Ross, and Ant & Dec, as well as a series of outtakes starring Will.i.am and Jeremy Clarkson. Plus, two toddlers run rings around Kay Burley, and Vicky Pattison forgets the name of the show she's presenting

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Sally is pleased to have spent the night with Greg. Ken tries to cheer Toyah up. Curly tells Anne not to sack Alma. Alec plans to move back to Southampton

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: The police try to get to the bottom of the kidnap drama and its shocking outcome. Kelly is full of remorse over Scott. Tricia tries to impress Richie with her literary knowledge

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kelly tells Viv she does not want to see Roy again. Chris is scared of going home. Zoe hears Viv call her a murderer

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Greengrass embarks on his most bizarre exploit yet - robbing a grave in Aidensfield churchyard to retrieve a stuffed sheep that has mistakenly been buried instead of its former owner. It sounds like something out of a macabre black comedy, but as usual there's a method in his madness - and a quick profit uppermost in his mind. Nick Berry and Bill Maynard star

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: The family return from holiday, and it seems as if they never went away. Seventies comedy, starring Sid James

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: When Larry decides to use someone else's name instead of his own, Robin finds himself entangled in a love triangle. Comedy series, with Richard O'Sullivan

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: When dairy worker Debbie Moffett (played by Martine McCutcheon) is crushed to death by a giant round of cheese at the home of the world-famous Midsomer Blue, Barnaby and Jones are soon on the case. Pathologist Kate Wilding discovers the victim's daughter was being expelled from the local prep school and it emerges Debbie died just hours after an argument at a parents' council meeting. Her phone leads the detectives to Oliver Ordish, with whom she was having an affair

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: An ex-soldier visits Aidensfield on holiday and his daughter strikes up a relationship with a local farm hand. Greengrass finds himself the owner of stolen cigarettes and Jo receives tragic news. Rural drama, with Bill Maynard and Nick Berry

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Richie continues to thwart Tricia's advances. Bernice tells Gavin that her mother is coming to stay. Kelly wants a party in the Woolpack to celebrate Roy's release

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kelly goes to meet Roy on his release from prison. Eric offers to go into partnership with Kathy at the diner. Roy tells Kelly they need to find a place of their own

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Kevin accuses Sally of pushing him out of the house. Toyah runs away from home. Alec is amazed when Rita tells him she does want to marry him

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Janice worries about Toyah's whereabouts. Sally asks Kevin to move back in and promises to try to make their marriage work. Alec and Rita agree to marry as soon as possible

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: When a wealthy businessman is killed in a mysterious explosion aboard his fishing boat and suspicion falls on his son, Wycliffe feels sure the real culprit is to be found elsewhere and unearths several locals with a motive for murder. Jack Shepherd stars as the Cornish copper, with Helen Masters and Jimmy Yuill

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A series of killings in Midsomer St Claire seem to be inspired by macabre images on a medieval fresco recently discovered in the church crypt. As the village prepares for storms and flooding, it appears that someone is using ancient torture methods to punish modern-day 'sinners'. With help from sidekick Charlie Nelson, Barnaby tries to get to the bottom of the case, and unearths a long-held secret that takes him by surprise. With Rebecca Front, Andrea Lowe, Vincent Regan and Roy Hudd

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: Isobel suggests using Downton as a place of convalescence following a distressing incident at the village hospital. Shell-shocked new valet Lang arrives as William gets his call-up papers, and Carson strives to keep standards high, but suffers a panic attack in front of the family. Lady Rosamund is eager to unearth Lavinia and Carlisle's secret after overhearing them arguing, and romance blossoms for both Edith and Mary. Costume drama, starring Jim Carter, Samantha Bond and Cal Macaninch

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: A body is found buried on the Northumberland moors which could be a missing teenager from the nearby rural community of Brindale. The girl had disappeared six weeks previously, and Vera has to break the news to her devastated family. However, the realisation that she died so close to home rather than at a music festival as originally thought creates tension among her neighbours, who are soon suspecting each other of murder. The detective delves into the vulnerable young woman's isolated existence to identify her killer

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: The detective is drawn into the strange murder of eminent art historian Professor Van Haren, who was really a former high-ranking Nazi hired by MI5 due to his expert knowledge of Soviet intelligence. Meanwhile, as Sam's husband Adam embarks on a busy career as an MP, she worries whether she will be able to share some big news with him. Crime drama set in the 1940s, starring Michael Kitchen and Honeysuckle Weeks, with Tamzin Outhwaite and Rupert Vansittart

programme.title: Victoria
programme.desc: Feature-length festive special. It is Christmas 1846 and Victoria is uncharacteristically sad as she prepares to face her first Christmas without her beloved Baroness Lehzen, while Albert transforms the palace into a magical festive wonderland as he is gripped by an obsession with staging the perfect family Christmas. But tensions between the couple mount when the prince invites house guests to join the celebrations. Meanwhile, Skerrett inherits a large estate from an estranged uncle. Starring Jenna Coleman and Tom Hughes

With help from sidekick Charlie Nelson, Barnaby tries to get to the bottom of the case, and unearths a long-held secret that takes him by surprise. With Rebecca Front, Andrea Lowe, Vincent Regan and Roy Hudd

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Nick helps a woman claiming she is being harassed by a mysterious stalker, and discovers the culprit is closer to home than anticipated. Greengrass gets up to his old tricks again - this time trying to persuade a group of wealthy Australians he is a long-lost relative. Drama, starring Nick Berry, Bill Maynard and Juliette Gruber.

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Roy's nightmares cause concern from Kelly. Zoe wants to face the village over the lorry affair. Roy tells Kelly that he is keen on having kids in the future

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Janice is upset that Toyah has gone looking for Ronnie. Vera fears she will end up homeless and jobless. Alec takes Rita shopping for wedding rings

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Janice and Les arrive in London and call on Ronnie. Kevin is surprised when Sally splashes out on his birthday. Jack and Vera agree to Alec buying them out of the pub

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: The detective and his team embark on a race against time to recapture a dangerous convict who has escaped from Bodmin prison - a case that leads them to an elaborate drugs racket. Starring Jack Shepherd, Helen Masters, Jimmy Yuill and Sean Chapman

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: When wealthy farmer Martin Strickland is discovered bound to a tree, doused in truffle oil and mauled to death by a wild boar, the investigation leads DCI Barnaby to tyrannical celebrity chef Ruth Cameron at the upmarket Wyvern House restaurant. However, secrets from the past surface after the murderer strikes again, and the repercussions are felt further than they were originally intended. With Neil Dudgeon, Neil McCaul, Sharon Small, Marc Elliott and Arabella Weir

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: The wounded officers arrive at Downton and Matthew suggests he bring General Sir Herbert Strutt to inspect the new convalescent home - but Branson decides to spoil the visit after failing his army medical. Meanwhile, William's return results in an awkward surprise for Daisy, and Violet and Rosamund discover Lavinia's past misdeeds. Costume drama, starring Dan Stevens, Allen Leech, Thomas Howes, Maggie Smith and Zoe Boyle

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: With Louisa visiting her mother in Spain to get some perspective on their marriage, Martin knows he needs to try to change for the sake of their relationship, so he endeavours to keep his promise to her to see a therapist. Aunt Ruth arranges an appointment for him with Dr Rachel Timoney, but a lifeboat training exercise causes problems for the doctor. Will he get back in time for his first session? Martin Clunes stars

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Al welcomes his first fishing guests, but an uninvited one means that a good review is definitely not guaranteed, while Bert decides to try to rejuvenate trade at the restaurant by joining an online voucher scheme. Morwenna asks Martin for a pay rise, and Louisa has to find a new babysitter to look after James Henry and reluctantly decides to offer Janice a trial. Penhale is excited to receive his new police issue Taser, but Portwenn does not exactly offer him plenty of opportunities to try it out. Comedy drama, starring Martin Clunes

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: Michael Kitchen returns to the role of Christopher Foyle, senior intelligence officer for MI5, in the first of three films. Foyle is drawn into the world of corrupt Nazi businessmen when a university professor is found murdered in a London park. When it transpires the victim was working as a translator in Nuremberg, the detective realises there are powerful people trying to conceal secrets from the Second World War. Meanwhile, Sam (Honeysuckle Weeks) volunteers for a risky undercover job, despite being pregnant. Guest stars include John Mahoney (Frasier), Jaime Winstone (Mad Dogs) and Nigel Lindsay (Four Lions)

programme.title: Victoria
programme.desc: The period drama resumes in 1848, a tumultuous year in which several European nations embraced revolution - placing the British monarchy under pressure. Victoria, Albert and their young family find themselves taking in refugees, when the queen's half-sister Feodora (Harlots' Kate Fleetwood) and a king who has been overthrown come to the palace seeking sanctuary. John Sessions and Laurence Fox (Lewis) join the cast as current and future prime ministers Lord John Russell and Lord Palmerston

programme.title: Victoria
programme.desc: As the Chartist movement turns violent, Victoria must decide whether to use force against her own people. Albert is convinced that the royal family must flee to the Isle of Wight, but Victoria is still weak from the recent birth of her sixth child, and is determined to stay in London regardless of the threat. Skerrett is torn between her old life and new when Francatelli buys a hotel, and Lord Palmerston comes to Sophie's rescue when her carriage is overturned. Louis Phillippe has outstayed his welcome, and Albert is forced to get rid of the unwanted guest

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: The age gap between Sid and his children becomes apparent when he returns home early from work. Comedy, starring Sid James

programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Long-running drama with the residents of England's most famous cobbled street

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Rita's shocked to find Sharon in her flat, having taken a beating from Harvey's gang for trying to warn Leanne. Summer is horrified to realise Billy and Todd think she has an eating disorder. Emma flirts with Curtis in the bistro

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: As Harvey's trial gets underway, Leanne enters the witness box. Carol calls at the Rovers saying she wants out of Double Glammy. Curtis is confused by Emma's noticeably cooler attitude and confides in Steve

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: As Fiz continues to mock Tyrone, he reveals he's booked them a mediation session. Sharon packs her bag and tells Rita that after giving evidence she fears for her life. With a potential buyer due, Daisy suggests to Jenny that she take out a loan and they buy the pub together

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Captain Hastings is staying with friends when wealthy heiress Mrs Inglethorp is murdered. He calls in Hercule Poirot to help him solve the baffling crime, which has the whole household under suspicion. Whodunit, starring David Suchet as the Belgian sleuth, with Hugh Fraser, Pauline Moran, Anthony Calf and Philip Jackson

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Two couples disappear in the tiny village of Monks Barton amid rumours of witchcraft and haunted woods. As Barnaby and Jones investigate, psychic Cyrus LeVanu clashes with cleric Wallace Stone over their different methods of 'healing' the woods, while other locals seem to be caught up in a stolen antiques racket. When a body is unearthed and the death toll rises, the detectives race to catch the killer by finding a connection between the missing couples and the thefts. Anton Lesser guest stars, with John Nettles and Jason Hughes

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: The detective suspects a connection between the death of a student with secret plans for advancement and the killing of well-known criminal Nicky Turnball, who was in Oxford to speak at the Union. As Lewis and Hathaway eliminate suspects from a long list of the crook's enemies, the investigation brings him into contact with old flame Diane - who also happens to be Turnball's wife. Kevin Whately stars, with Laurence Fox and Gina McKee

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: A ritual killing in a village church points to the work of a satanic cult, but the detective thinks local animosity might actually hold the key to the mystery. Detective drama, starring Jack Shepherd

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Captain Hastings and the Belgian supersleuth investigate a baffling gangland murder which draws them both into the underworld of London's Little Italy. Miss Lemon turns out to have a link with the victim's valet. Whodunit, starring David Suchet and Hugh Fraser

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A prestigious golf club is the setting for murder when a player is bludgeoned to death on the 13th hole. Barnaby and Jones discover gambling, illegal money-lending and assault are rife among the snobbish members, who are also dismissive of the local villagers and only allow them on the course for a few hours a week. Before long, another body turns up, making the detectives doubly determined to catch this terror of the fairways. John Nettles and Jason Hughes star, with guest appearances by Nicholas Le Prevost, John Standing and real-life golfer Sam Torrance

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby's secret past as a spy is revealed when a former agent is killed after a cricket match between the villagers and the gentry. The dead man's wounds appear to have been inflicted by a giant claw, triggering rumours of a 'beast of Midsomer', and before long, another body turns up with similar injuries. Barnaby then realises the incidents are linked to the time the local network of spies spent in Cold War Berlin. John Nettles and Jason Hughes star, with guest appearances by Anna Massey, Peter Davison and Neil Stuke

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The murder of a journalist has chilling echoes of a 1960s horror film starring Midsomer's own Stella Harris. When the actress's more famous sister Diana returns after a 40-year family rift, the body count rises, with each crime reminiscent of a movie plot - giving Barnaby and Jones what looks like a disturbing case of life imitating art. Neil Dudgeon and Jason Hughes star, with Harriet Walter and Sinead Cusack

programme.title: Sanditon
programme.desc: It's the final visit to the Sunday night period drama, giving viewers a chance to find out how writer Andrew Davies has brought his adaptation of Jane Austen's unfinished novel to an end. On the night of the Midsummer Ball, romantic interests are finally resolved. However, the fate of Sanditon is in the balance when events conspire to leave Charlotte and the Parker family facing down impossible choices. Rose Williams stars as the feisty heroine, with Theo James, Anne Reid and Kris Marshall

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: When Larry decides to use someone else's name instead of his own, Robin finds himself entangled in a love triangle. Comedy series, with Richard O'Sullivan

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Les rows with Toyah over the bomb hoax call. Greg urges Sally to stand firm and not sign the mortgage papers. Alec is furious when Vera docks his pay

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Sally is pleased to have spent the night with Greg. Ken tries to cheer Toyah up. Curly tells Anne not to sack Alma. Alec plans to move back to Southampton

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: The police try to get to the bottom of the kidnap drama and its shocking outcome. Kelly is full of remorse over Scott. Tricia tries to impress Richie with her literary knowledge

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kelly tells Viv she does not want to see Roy again. Chris is scared of going home. Zoe hears Viv call her a murderer

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Greengrass embarks on his most bizarre exploit yet - robbing a grave in Aidensfield churchyard to retrieve a stuffed sheep that has mistakenly been buried instead of its former owner. It sounds like something out of a macabre black comedy, but as usual there's a method in his madness - and a quick profit uppermost in his mind. Nick Berry and Bill Maynard star

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: The family return from holiday, and it seems as if they never went away. Seventies comedy, starring Sid James

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: When Larry decides to use someone else's name instead of his own, Robin finds himself entangled in a love triangle. Comedy series, with Richard O'Sullivan

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: When dairy worker Debbie Moffett (played by Martine McCutcheon) is crushed to death by a giant round of cheese at the home of the world-famous Midsomer Blue, Barnaby and Jones are soon on the case. Pathologist Kate Wilding discovers the victim's daughter was being expelled from the local prep school and it emerges Debbie died just hours after an argument at a parents' council meeting. Her phone leads the detectives to Oliver Ordish, with whom she was having an affair

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: An ex-soldier visits Aidensfield on holiday and his daughter strikes up a relationship with a local farm hand. Greengrass finds himself the owner of stolen cigarettes and Jo receives tragic news. Rural drama, with Bill Maynard and Nick Berry

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Richie continues to thwart Tricia's advances. Bernice tells Gavin that her mother is coming to stay. Kelly wants a party in the Woolpack to celebrate Roy's release

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kelly goes to meet Roy on his release from prison. Eric offers to go into partnership with Kathy at the diner. Roy tells Kelly they need to find a place of their own

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Kevin accuses Sally of pushing him out of the house. Toyah runs away from home. Alec is amazed when Rita tells him she does want to marry him

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Janice worries about Toyah's whereabouts. Sally asks Kevin to move back in and promises to try to make their marriage work. Alec and Rita agree to marry as soon as possible

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: When a wealthy businessman is killed in a mysterious explosion aboard his fishing boat and suspicion falls on his son, Wycliffe feels sure the real culprit is to be found elsewhere and unearths several locals with a motive for murder. Jack Shepherd stars as the Cornish copper, with Helen Masters and Jimmy Yuill

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A series of killings in Midsomer St Claire seem to be inspired by macabre images on a medieval fresco recently discovered in the church crypt. As the village prepares for storms and flooding, it appears that someone is using ancient torture methods to punish modern-day 'sinners'. With help from sidekick Charlie Nelson, Barnaby tries to get to the bottom of the case, and unearths a long-held secret that takes him by surprise. With Rebecca Front, Andrea Lowe, Vincent Regan and Roy Hudd

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: Isobel suggests using Downton as a place of convalescence following a distressing incident at the village hospital. Shell-shocked new valet Lang arrives as William gets his call-up papers, and Carson strives to keep standards high, but suffers a panic attack in front of the family. Lady Rosamund is eager to unearth Lavinia and Carlisle's secret after overhearing them arguing, and romance blossoms for both Edith and Mary. Costume drama, starring Jim Carter, Samantha Bond and Cal Macaninch

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: A body is found buried on the Northumberland moors which could be a missing teenager from the nearby rural community of Brindale. The girl had disappeared six weeks previously, and Vera has to break the news to her devastated family. However, the realisation that she died so close to home rather than at a music festival as originally thought creates tension among her neighbours, who are soon suspecting each other of murder. The detective delves into the vulnerable young woman's isolated existence to identify her killer

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: The detective is drawn into the strange murder of eminent art historian Professor Van Haren, who was really a former high-ranking Nazi hired by MI5 due to his expert knowledge of Soviet intelligence. Meanwhile, as Sam's husband Adam embarks on a busy career as an MP, she worries whether she will be able to share some big news with him. Crime drama set in the 1940s, starring Michael Kitchen and Honeysuckle Weeks, with Tamzin Outhwaite and Rupert Vansittart

programme.title: Victoria
programme.desc: Feature-length festive special. It is Christmas 1846 and Victoria is uncharacteristically sad as she prepares to face her first Christmas without her beloved Baroness Lehzen, while Albert transforms the palace into a magical festive wonderland as he is gripped by an obsession with staging the perfect family Christmas. But tensions between the couple mount when the prince invites house guests to join the celebrations. Meanwhile, Skerrett inherits a large estate from an estranged uncle. Starring Jenna Coleman and Tom Hughes

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: Simon Templar is persuaded to shadow a defecting civil servant who has been duped into carrying confidential papers to East Germany. Guest starring Ronald Hines

programme.title: The Casebook of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: A racehorse trainer asks Holmes to investigate the mysterious disappearance of one of his cash-strapped employer's many creditors. Victorian whodunit, starring Jeremy Brett and Edward Hardwicke, with a young Jude Law in an early supporting role

programme.title: The Avengers
programme.desc: Steed is called in to investigate when Sir Gerald Bancroft and his Rolls-Royce mysteriously disappear while being escorted around a military base. Light-hearted drama, starring Patrick Macnee, Diana Rigg and Francis Matthews

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Bodie and Doyle infiltrate a gang smuggling art treasures and industrial secrets out of the country and selling them on to foreign powers. As the investigation proceeds, the duo discover the illicit operation is being run by a country squire in his stately home. Drama, starring Martin Shaw and Lewis Collins

programme.title: UEFA Euro 2020 Highlights
programme.desc: Reshmin Chowdhury presents actiojn from Slovakia v Spain at Estadio La Cartuja in Seville and Sweden v Poland at Saint Petersburg Stadium in Group E, and Portugal v France at Puskas Arena in Budapest and Germany v Hungary at at Fußball Arena München in Group F, as both groups concluded. With analysis from Andros Townsend, Robert Earnshaw and John Collins

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur gets an illegal car phone, while Terry dates the wealthy daughter of a hamburger king who suddenly goes bankrupt. Vintage comedy drama, guest starring Patrick Malahide and Peter Capaldi alongside George Cole and Dennis Waterman

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: Simon Templar sets out on the trail of a former Nazi believed to have taken refuge in South America. Vintage drama, guest starring Francesca Annis, Roger Delgado, John Barrie and Maurice Kaufmann

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Extreme angler Jeremy Wade travels to the foothills of the Himalayas to investigate stories of deadly attacks by the man-eating goonch catfish, tracking down the species to establish if its methods of killing are as gruesome as reports suggest

programme.title: The Casebook of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: A desperate girl contacts Holmes and asks him to clear her boyfriend of killing his father, a farmer found bludgeoned to death beside a lake. Period drama, starring Jeremy Brett , Peter Vaughan and James Purefoy

programme.title: The Avengers
programme.desc: Steed and Mrs Peel investigate reports of a traitor in the ranks, and end up facing the heart-breaking prospect of bidding each other farewell. Patrick Macnee stars, with Diana Rigg making her final appearance as Emma and Linda Thorson taking over as Steed's latest partner Tara King

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: A recently released convict causes chaos when he takes a group of hospital patients hostage. Bodie and Doyle try to get to him before he can start shooting. Drama, starring Martin Shaw, Lewis Collins and Gordon Jackson

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur makes a hasty agreement to stage a burglary after his tailor's staff go on strike demanding more pay. The bumbling safecracker he hires blows himself up, leaving Arthur and Terry with the task of liberating him from hospital before his bandages are removed and the duo can be linked with the botched crime

programme.title: The Motorbike Show
programme.desc: Henry Cole rides a 1960s drag bike at North Weald airstrip, tells the story of the Brough Superior and continues his project to strip down an ivy-covered Honda

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh returns with the celebrity version of the team quiz show. As ever, four star contestants are challenged to pit their wits against one of the show's ruthless quiz experts, in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds for their charity of choice. This time, Absolute Radio's Christian O'Connell, singer, TV presenter and vocal coach Carrie Grant, Channel 5 children's presenter Naomi Wilkinson and I, Daniel Blake star Dave Johns each try to race down the game board without being caught, before joining forces for the final chase

programme.title: Quantum of Solace (2008)
programme.desc: James Bond sets out on a personal mission of vengeance as he pursues the secret criminal organisation Quantum. His search leads him to the head of an ecological corporation, who is plotting to orchestrate a coup in a Latin American country to get his hands on a precious natural resource. Spy thriller sequel, starring Daniel Craig, Olga Kurylenko, Judi Dench, Gemma Arterton and Mathieu Amalric

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: A crook flees justice in the guise of a nun, hotly pursued by Simon Templar - who suddenly finds himself having to take a crash-course in the finer points of flying a stolen experimental plane. Drama, guest starring William Gaunt

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: The bull shark can tolerate fresh water, allowing it to travel far up rivers and into close contact with humans. Biologist and extreme angler Jeremy Wade investigates the death of a man and a bloody attack on a racehorse as he tries to establish the truth about the notorious fish

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: America boasts a species of fish reputed to have committed a number of violent attacks on humans. The alligator gar is said to be as vicious as a shark and as large as an alligator. Biologist and extreme angler Jeremy Wade heads to Texas on a hunt to uncover the truth about the notorious creature

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Biologist and extreme angler Jeremy Wade travels to Germany to investigate tales of a beast that has been on the prowl in European waters for the past 700 years. His investigations take him to Spain, where he discovers first-hand that as a consequence of a science experiment gone wrong, the Wels catfish is growing to man-eating proportions and is capable of everything depicted in medieval records

programme.title: The Casebook of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: In the most dangerous assignment of his career, Holmes is asked to stop the marriage of Violet Merville to Baron Gruner - a wealthy collector of objets d'art and beautiful women, whom the sleuth knows to be a murderer. Victorian detective drama, guest starring Anthony Valentine with Jeremy Brett

programme.title: The Avengers
programme.desc: Tara and Steed try and put all the pieces together when people start turning up dead in unique locations with a jigsaw puzzle piece placed on their persons. Peter Jeffrey guest stars

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: When a foreign student, an apparent cocaine trafficker, is shot dead, Cowley tasks Bodie and Doyle with targetting teenage traffickers who have diplomatic ties with South America. Drama, starring Martin Shaw, Lewis Collins, Gordon Jackson and Alice Krige

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur must find a way of paying a £20,000 tax bill immediately, but fortunately Dave has a drastic solution - faking a drowning accident. Chisholm is suspicious as there is no body, while Arthur is spotted hiding out in a hotel. Vintage comedy drama, starring George Cole and Dennis Waterman

programme.title: The Motorbike Show
programme.desc: Henry Cole joins 3,500 other riders on the Bike for Life ride out, and meets bike builder extraordinaire, Alan Milyard, who tells the story of the Triumph Bonneville

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as celebrity contestants Mark Billingham, Aldo Zilli, Lucy Pargeter and Joey Essex pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: ITV Football Classics
programme.desc: Gabriel Clarke introduces action from the memorable Group A match, which came from Wembley Stadium. Bob Wilson presented the coverage, with analysis from John Barnes and Jack Charlton, and commentary by Brian Moore and Kevin Keegan

programme.title: Terminator 2: Judgment Day (1991)
programme.desc: A reprogrammed killing machine is sent back in time to the 20th century to protect John Connor, a teenage boy whose destiny is to save the human race. After breaking John's mother out of a mental institution, the pair set out to avert a future nuclear war - pursued by an advanced prototype terminator with shape-changing powers. Sci-fi action adventure sequel, starring Arnold Schwarzenegger, Linda Hamilton, Edward Furlong and Robert Patrick

programme.title: The Waterboy (1998)
programme.desc: An American football team's waterboy puts up with constant ridicule from the players. However, he has to keep his blossoming sporting career a secret from his overprotective mother. Comedy, with Adam Sandler, Kathy Bates, Henry Winkler and Fairuza Balk

programme.title: Tour de France Live
programme.desc: La Course by Le Tour. Coverage of the eighth staging of the one-day women's race, which consists of a 107.4m route from Brest to Landerneau. Gary Imlach presents, with analysis from Chris Boardman, reports by Daniel Friebe and commentary from Ned Boulting

programme.title: Tour de France Live
programme.desc: Coverage of stage one of the most prestigious race on the calendar, featuring a 198km route from Brest to Landerneau. Gary Imlach presents, with analysis from Chris Boardman and Peter Kennaugh, reports by Daniel Friebe and Matthew Rendell, and commentary from Ned Boulting and David Millar

programme.title: Sport Filler Shows (2020)
programme.desc: A look back at memorable stagings of the race from Epsom Downs. Featuring brilliant performances from Shergar to Sea The Stars, plus interviews with personalities from the worlds of sport and entertainment

programme.title: Mr Bean
programme.desc: The hapless fool sits an exam, but when he realises he has studied the wrong maths equations, it is already too late. He also tries to stifle a sneeze in church. Rowan Atkinson stars in the first episode of the celebrated comedy, with Richard Briers, Rudolph Walker and Howard Goodall

programme.title: Mr Bean
programme.desc: The buffoon wreaks havoc when he goes shopping and dines at an exclusive restaurant - and there is an unfortunate incident when he meets a member of the royal family at a film premiere. Rowan Atkinson stars

programme.title: Junk and Disorderly
programme.desc: Vintage restorers Henry Cole and Sam Lovegrove challenge themselves to make money out of buying and selling other people's unwanted automotive junk and classic collectibles. They begin at Bristol Classic Car Show, where they acquire a quirky car from the 1970s, but making a quick profit does not prove easy

programme.title: Space Cowboys (2000)
programme.desc: A former Air Force test pilot, who was sidelined from space exploration because of his difficult character, is recruited 40 years later to help America's post-Cold War Russian allies. Teaming up with three fellow veteran airmen, he uses his expertise in navigation to stop a malfunctioning Soviet satellite from crashing to Earth. Adventure, directed by and starring Clint Eastwood. With Tommy Lee Jones, James Garner, Donald Sutherland and James Cromwell

With a budget of £300,000 Danni Menzies is on hand to show them five properties around the south of the island

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun: Where Are They Now?
programme.desc: Catching up with a London couple who opened a successful B&B in Spain and a family whose members swapped Blackpool for France to run a classic-car parts business

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Nottingham, long-time married couple Karen and Nigel kick off the competition with a Bajan menu inspired by Nigel's West Indian heritage. However, banter buddies Jo and Charlie aren't loving the Caribbean cuisine. On day two twenty-somethings Beth and Clem attempt to cook restaurant-standard food, with a celebrity twist. However, not everyone is a fan of the fine dining experience. Finally, Jo and Charlie rely on a night of Greek gastro goodies to win the £1,000 prize on offer

programme.title: The Simpsons

programme.title: Channel 4 News

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Noel treats chocolate Labrador Inca, who has a history of developmental elbow disease and osteoarthritis that has led to her elbow joints becoming worn out. Two-year-old Cavapoo Nala, who has hip dysplasia, and Great Dane Charles, who is unsteady on his feet due to a squashed spinal cord, are also on the patients list. Charles' owner Steve knows only too well that spinal surgery is a hugely serious undertaking, as he himself had multiple surgeries following a bike accident a decade earlier

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: Make Do and Mend
programme.desc: Dick and Angel Strawbridge help Kate and Erik restore their 150-year-old sash window and turn their small landing into a beautiful space for all the family that not only looks amazing and brings the past back to life, but saves thousands of pounds. Angel reveals the art of creating repeat patterns on fabric as a unique solution to Jo and family's desire to display their precious vintage family photos in something other than a frame. Dick solves Suzanna and Luke's conundrum of how to turn a treasured wardrobe into a showcase for shoes, by designing ingenious shelving inspired by a visit to the kitchen

programme.title: We Are Lady Parts
programme.desc: Publicly shamed, shunned by her university friends and out of Lady Parts, Amina is at an all-time low, so is grateful when Noor offers to help her get back on track - firstly by finding a respectable man to marry who will overlook her transgressions. Meanwhile, Momtaz takes it upon herself to get the band back together. Comedy, with Anjana Vasan, Juliette Motamed, Lucie Shorthouse and Sarah Kameela Impey

programme.title: Celebrity Gogglebox
programme.desc: Nick and Liv Grimshaw, Denise van Outen and Eddie Boxshall, Mo Gilligan and Babatunde Aleshe, Shaun Ryder and Bez, Martin and Roman Kemp, Gyles Brandreth and Maureen Lipman, Iain Stirling and Laura Whitmore, Sunetra Sarker and Georgia Taylor, Lorraine Kelly and Rosie Smith, Melanie C and her brother Paul, Clare Balding and Alice Arnold, Chris Eubank Sr and Chris Eubank Jr, and Eamonn Holmes and Ruth Langsford appraise shows including Clarkson's Farm, Snakes on a Plane, Fresh, Fried and Crispy, Lupin, 10 Years Younger in 10 Days and Tiny World. Plus, news coverage of the G7 summit and a story about a man swallowed by a whale

programme.title: Love, Simon (2018)
programme.desc: Coming-of-age comedy drama about a secretly gay high-school student who begins to fall for an anonymous classmate after exchanging emails with the boy, known as 'Blue'. While trying to discover Blue's identity, Simon must also find himself. Starring Nick Robinson, Jennifer Garner and Josh Duhamel

programme.title: Ramsay's 24 Hours to Hell and Back
programme.desc: Gordon travels to Greenville, Mississippi, to save fine dining establishment Sherman's Restaurant, which has been a presence in the town for 30 years. The chef and his team have just 24 hours to revamp the premises, clean and renovate the kitchen and introduce a new menu featuring update versions of the restaurant's staple dishes, working around the clock to teach the staff how to prepare them

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Rugby enthusiast Andy and dental nurse Holly kick off the competition in Yorkshire with a Mexican fiesta - a fun-filled evening, but with a few blunders in the kitchen. Day two sees married couple Mitesh and Jess cooking up an Indian-inspired meal, but with a couple of spice-fearing guests, they have a tough crowd to please. Finally, it's the turn of Yorkshire couple Paul and Mandy to host with some 'proper grub'. After two nights of Paul larking around he has a few surprises in and out of the kitchen to try to secure the £1,000 prize

programme.title: Dispatches
programme.desc: Over the past two decades, more than half a million Eritreans have fled their home in eastern Africa, escaping one of the most repressive and secretive dictatorships in the world. Reporting in Eritrea is almost impossible but for more than five years, Evan Williams has been gathering secretly shot footage from inside the country and interviewing some of those who have escaped. Here he documents allegations of torture, arbitrary detention and indefinite forced conscription into military service

programme.title: Iris Prize Best British Shorts: Go Home Polish
programme.desc: Angered by graffiti scribbled in a British backstreet demanding 'Go Home Polish', a photographer embarks on a 1,000-mile walk back to his birthplace in search of home. Directed by Ian Smith

programme.title: Unreported World
programme.desc: The brides and grooms defying the jihadist militants laying siege to the city of Maiduguri in Nigeria, spiritual home of Boko Haram, a terrorist group at the centre of an insurgency that has killed over 36,000 people and displaced more than two million. With Covid lockdowns over and the militants driven out to north-eastern Nigeria, Yousra Elbagir discovers a city determined to reclaim the joy of weddings despite the ongoing risk of kidnappings and attacks. The reporter hears stories of loss and desperation, but also meets the residents determined to repair mental scars and rebuild Maiduguri for its people

programme.title: Iris Prize Best British Shorts: Rhiw Goch (On the Red Hill)
programme.desc: The intimate story of a Welsh farmhouse that was passed down a generation thanks to an unlikely friendship. Directed by Anna Winstone

programme.desc: Charlie Luxton helps more people as they build affordable dream homes. programme.title: Steph's Packed Lunch

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun: Where Are They Now?
programme.desc: How business is going for the British hairdresser who gambled everything on a salon in Spain, catching up with a couple looking forward to downsizing in France and a return to two bar owners on the Costa del Sol who became victims of their own success

programme.desc: Lovebirds Gabz and Pete kick off the competition in and around Nottingham by cooking the food they fell in love eating together. On day two Sushma and Gulab's traditional Indian banquet goes down a storm, at least until a sweet milky pudding arrives. Finally, country-dwelling partners John and Shaun serve up a home-grown menu, but not everything is as locally sourced as they had hoped

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Moe desperately wants to raise cash to lavish on his new girlfriend, so Homer rashly agrees to help him defraud his car insurance company - but his bumbling attempts to destroy the vehicle put him behind bars. Featuring the guest voice of Helen Hunt

programme.desc: Tony tells Scott that he needs to keep his distance, and a concerned Romeo tells Sylver that Cher is obsessing over her social media accounts again

programme.title: Channel 4 News

programme.title: Unreported World
programme.desc: Seyi Rhodes reports on the migrants being beaten back from the European Union by border guards on the notorious Balkan Route. A once-welcoming Europe is now closing its doors, and Serbia has become a bottleneck for thousands of people trying to get through the increasingly hostile route. Rhodes meets beaten and bruised men trying to leave Serbia, and witnesses the families living in government camps too frightened to make the journey. He also hears how the animosity is fuelling a small but growing right-wing political movement

programme.title: Great Canal Journeys
programme.desc: Sheila Hancock and broadcaster Gyles Brandreth tackle their first manual lock and some terrifyingly tight turns, but find themselves relaxing into life at 4mph, and marvel at how secret histories reveal themselves. They visit the Royal Gunpowder Mills, which built its own canal system to transport volatile ammunition - vital to Britain's success in both world wars

programme.title: Celebrity Gogglebox
programme.desc: Nick and Liv Grimshaw, Denise van Outen and Eddie Boxshall, Mo Gilligan and Babatunde Aleshe, Shaun Ryder and Bez, Martin and Roman Kemp, Gyles Brandreth and Maureen Lipman, Lorraine Kelly and Rosie Smith, Chris Eubank Sr and Chris Eubank Jr, and Eamonn Holmes and Ruth Langsford assess the week's biggest news stories, hard-hitting documentaries, laughable comedies and gritty dramas

programme.title: The Last Leg

programme.title: Girls Trip (2017)
programme.desc: A lifestyle guru is offered the chance to be a keynote speaker at a music festival in New Orleans. She decides to invite three old friends she has drifted apart from, hoping to turn a working weekend into a wild vacation and a chance to rebuild their bonds. Horror sequel, starring James Allen McCune, Callie Hernandez, Brandon Scott, Valorie Curry, Corbin Reid and Wes Robinson

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Data analyst John Tower hosts the second dinner party in north Kent, and lets his passion for military re-enactment show when he barks orders at the table. However, not all the guests enjoy his fatty pork stew and psychedelic dessert, and two argue over dating etiquette

programme.title: Come Dine with Me

programme.title: Come Dine with Me

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Party-loving Lisa Townend hosts the final dinner party in north Kent, and hopes that a menu full of French fancies and innovative entertainment in the form of human roulette, will be enough to impress her guests and see her crowned winner of the £1,000 cash prize

programme.title: Building the Dream
programme.desc: Charlie Luxton is in Surrey with Chris and Gemma, who are aiming to build an Arts and Crafts-inspired home of grand proportions. But their determination to do most of the work themselves while juggling a family means progress grinds to a halt

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Frank catches a football at a college game and refuses to give it back. He tries instead to auction it over the radio for $10,000 - much to the horror of Ray, who tries to make him feel guilty for being so mercenary

programme.desc: Ray discovers the leader of local group the Frontier Girls is intending to sell a record number of cookies in his area, and sets out to beat her at her own game. Comedy, starring Ray Romano and Amy Aquino

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Robert is ecstatic when he is recommended for a job with the FBI, until it starts to look like Marie's intrusive ways may scupper his chances

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Leonard and Penny ask Howard and Bernadette for advice when they argue about money. Comedy, starring Johnny Galecki, Jim Parsons and Kaley Cuoco

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer rents a Western for the family to watch, but is disappointed when it turns out to be a musical, so the family reminds him of musical highlights from past episodes. Featuring the guest voice of George Harrison

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: A sleazy carnival worker and his son lose their jobs thanks to Homer, and end up moving in with the Simpson family - whose home they proceed to take over and completely wreck. With the guest voice of Jim Varney

programme.title: The Simpsons

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Krusty's ancient jokes and politically incorrect impressions go down like a lead balloon at a charity comedy festival - so he decides to spice up his act by adding more alternative, adult-oriented material. programme.title: Four in a Bed

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final visit is to Phil Gilbert's Oak Tree Lane campsite in Bratton Clovelly, Devon, where the guests are wowed by the traditional yurts, only for arguments to cause tension

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners meet for the last time to find out what they have been paid and settle some scores. The Edge Accommodation is first, and host John wants to shut the door on some criticism. Next, Sally and Teresa want to challenge John over his comments on their hosting skills

programme.title: Live International Rugby Union
programme.desc: British & Irish Lions v Japan (Kick-off 3.00pm). Lee McKenzie presents coverage of the Lions' first match on home soil since 2005, held at Murrayfield. This contest serves as a warm-up ahead of their tour of South Africa and the Cherry Blossoms, who reached the quarter-finals of the 2019 World Cup, could provide a stern test of their credentials as Warren Gatland assesses his squad. With analysis from Ian McGeechan, Sam Warburton and Bryan Habana

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Jasmine Harman goes house hunting on Spain's Costa Blanca with Barnsley couple Paul and Lisa, who cannot wait to buy their first property together in the ever-popular resort of Torrevieja. They have three children and seven grandchildren wanting to visit, so the pressure is on Jasmine to find a holiday home the whole family can enjoy. With a £72,000 budget, she shows them five options - but will any measure up to their expectations?

programme.title: Channel 4 News
programme.desc: Including sport and weather

programme.title: Formula 1
programme.desc: Steve Jones presents action from the battle for pole from the eighth round of the season, held at Red Bull Ring in Spielberg, Austria. This is the first of two Grands Prix to take place at this venue in consecutive weeks and the drivers will have been eager to get to grips with the track as quickly as possible. With analysis from Mark Webber and David Coulthard

programme.title: Finding Your Feet (2017)
programme.desc: A wealthy woman's privileged existence is shattered when she discovers her husband is having an affair with her best friend. Seeking solace, she turns to her estranged sister, who lives in a London council estate, and finds a new lease of life in joining her community dance class. Comedy, starring Imelda Staunton, Celia Imrie, Timothy Spall and Joanna Lumley

programme.title: Mission: Impossible - Ghost Protocol (2011)
programme.desc: Secret agent Ethan Hunt and his team are implicated in a bombing at the Kremlin, forcing the American president to declare them fugitives. Action adventure sequel, starring Tom Cruise, Jeremy Renner, Simon Pegg and Paula Patton

programme.title: Survivor (2015)

Thriller, starring Milla Jovovich, Pierce Brosnan and Dylan McDermott

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA

programme.title: Witches: A Century of Murder
programme.desc: Suzannah Lipscomb's investigation into the notorious 17th-century witch trials concludes with a profile of Matthew Hopkins, the opportunistic puritan witch-hunter who transformed paranoia about Satanism into a lucrative business as he hanged hundreds of people for crimes they could not possibly have committed. Making use of contemporary reports, Suzannah paints a portrait of a man who believed he could cross any boundary and break any law to achieve his goals. Naming himself 'Witchfinder General', Hopkins toured the country charging fees for his services, but Suzannah's research reveals that ultimately, the public's distaste for witches was not as strong as its hatred of being conned by charlatans. Hopkins would eventually die of tuberculosis in relative obscurity, but his malign legacy lived on across the Atlantic, as witch-hunts began in Salem, Massachusetts

programme.title: Divine Designs
programme.desc: Paul Binski visits Liverpool's two cathedrals, which lie at either end of Hope Street. Despite having been built within 60 years of each other they represent starkly contrasting styles, the Anglican edifice being a vast tribute to neo-Gothic design and its Catholic counterpart a typical example of 1960s modernism

programme.title: Scotland: A Year in the Wild
programme.desc: An in-depth look at the wildlife and natural elements that are abundant during the springtime in Scotland. A female mountain hare tests the virility of male contenders by letting them chase her over the mountains. Spring is also the season when emperor moths emerge from their silk cocoons. The males immediately start trying to find a mate, picking up the females' scent. Back in Shetland, spring plankton blooms have drawn in humpback whales, overlooked by millions ofseabirds, including 25,000 puffins

programme.title: Gregg Wallace's Big Weekend Away
programme.desc: Gregg heads to Munich, the capital of the German state of Bavaria, to discover a city that has arguably done more to shape the image of Germany than any other. Over the course of three days, he discovers what it means to be Bavarian and how important history, traditions and, of course, food is to that identity. He gets stuck into pretzels, schnitzels and mountains of pork as he eats his way around the city. Before he leaves the city, Gregg takes home a special souvenir - his very own set of lederhosen
