programme.desc: Part one of two. Suzannah Lipscomb presents a profile of the last Russian tsar and empress, beginning with a look at their unconventional courtship and religious upbringings

programme.title: Nicholas and Alexandra: The Letters
programme.desc: Part two of two. Suzannah Lipscomb examines the lives of the royal couple during the revolution and their eventual execution, and considers how history has come to view them

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Princess Margaret: The Rebel Royal
programme.desc: Part one of two. Documentary profile of the Queen's sister, whose life and loves reflected the social and sexual revolution that transformed Britain during the 20th century. With archive footage and interviews, the series follows Margaret's life as she redefined the image of the modern princess, and begins with her birth in 1930. The abdication of her uncle propelled Margaret to second in line to the throne, but her life would be shaped by dramatic social change as Britain entered a more egalitarian era after the Second World War

programme.title: Princess Margaret: The Rebel Royal
programme.desc: Part two of two. Experts analyse the Princess's marriage to Lord Snowdon at the start of the 1960s. At home with celebrities and artists, they're riding the wave of a cultural and sexual revolution that's transforming Britain. The mystique of monarchy is being increasingly undermined by the emergence of a more open and egalitarian society

programme.title: Forbidden History
programme.desc: The Great Pyramid at Giza in Egypt is a Wonder of the World - but when was it built, for what purpose and by who?

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: A look onboard the $200million Black Pearl, which is the largest sailing yacht in the world and has been built to sail faster than any ship of its size

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: A look at the Qinghai-Tibet line, the highest railway in the world, providing an insight into the engineering challenges faced at high altitude and in bitterly cold temperatures

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo concludes his journey from Newcastle to the Leicestershire town of Melton Mowbray. Along the way he learns the secrets of Stilton cheese, discovers how trains transformed the traditional British pursuit of fox-hunting and tries to make a pork pie

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo embarks on the next leg of his journey, travelling from London Bridge through Kent and around the scenic south coast to Hastings. He begins by visiting the Royal Observatory to learn how Greenwich Mean Time standardised time on the railways, goes through the world's first underwater tunnel at Rotherhithe and explores the historic dockyards at Chatham

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from Eltham Palace in South London. Finds include a collection of Gerry and Sylvia Anderson puppets, two unknown paintings by Mexican Marxist artist Diego Rivera, whose work documented working class culture in the 1960s, a well-preserved mammoth tooth, a newspaper printing plate telling the story of the moon landings, and letters written by an Army officer captured by the IRA in the 1920s

programme.title: Impossible Railways
programme.desc: How engineers have built railways across mountains, including tunnels through the Swiss Alps and an Australian track that runs up a near-vertical cliff face

programme.title: Ganges
programme.desc: Exploring the story of the river, following its course from the Himalayas to their foothills. The programme reveals its enormous influence on the day-to-day living of a continent, how it has shaped Indian culture and religion and why it attracts a vast array of wildlife including snow leopards, Asiatic brown bears and bearded vultures

programme.title: The Life of Mammals
programme.desc: David Attenborough swims alongside sea otters, discovering he is no match for their natural agility in the water, while in Florida he takes a dip with manatees for the first time and observes their feeding habits. On the coast of California, he encounters the largest mammals on the planet, blue whales, and reveals how they, along with porpoises and dolphins, learned to court, mate and give birth in the water

programme.title: The Day When
programme.desc: How the Nazi invasion of Russia in 1941 would lead directly to Hitler's ultimate downfall four years later

programme.title: The Great War in Numbers
programme.desc: The key events of 1916, examining the unprecedented levels of recruitment by both sides, and the thousands of deaths in the battles of Verdun and the Jutland Peninsula

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from Eltham Palace in South London. Finds include a collection of Gerry and Sylvia Anderson puppets, two unknown paintings by Mexican Marxist artist Diego Rivera, whose work documented working class culture in the 1960s, a well-preserved mammoth tooth, a newspaper printing plate telling the story of the moon landings, and letters written by an Army officer captured by the IRA in the 1920s

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo embarks on the next leg of his journey from London Bridge to Hastings, exploring the lives of Victorian hop-pickers along the way. He also examines the history of Maidstone's paper industry, and discovers the role railways played in transforming cricket into a national sport

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues his journey through Kent. He starts in Canterbury, where he finds out how the city's cathedral was saved from the Second World War air raids, then goes whelk fishing in Whitstable and explores the origins of a seaside swim in Margate

programme.title: Steam Train Britain
programme.desc: On the Welsh Highland Railway, trainee Claire Oates takes her driving test, while chef Alex and manager Michelle prepare a dinner for 130 people on the East Lancashire railway

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: There is a triple collection of desirable cars in store for Derek when an MG Midget, an Austin-Healey 'frogeye' Sprite and an RS2000 come up for auction

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Dave's son Jack heads to Cheshire to collect two cars with a 35-year age difference, and luckily for Mathewsons, Lucy knows exactly what's been brought in for auction

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: A 1970 Excalibur Roadstar, once owned by Roy Orbison, arrives at Thornton-le-Dale and Derek heads to County Durham to collect a 1935 Austin 7 Ulster

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Two iconic classics find their way to the Mathewsons' auction room. Dave heads to north Manchester to pick up an Austin A30 and a Morris 8E that's had an alternative paint job

programme.title: Private Lives
programme.desc: Dr Tracy Borman, Chief Curator at Historic Royal Palaces, goes behind closed doors to reveal the private lives of six historical figures, beginning with Princess Margaret. Dr Borman discovers the real reason Margaret's relationship with RAF officer Peter Townsend came to an end, uncovers her disastrous marriage to Antony Snowden and discovers more about her love of music and devotion to duty

programme.title: Private Lives
programme.desc: Dr Tracy Borman, Chief Curator at Historic Royal Palaces, explores Edward VIII's childhood with his overbearing father, reveals how the First World War saw him grow up in more ways than one, and explores his apparent desire for married women. She also uncovers the truth about Edward's abdication of the throne, and Wallis Simpson's infidelity, and reveals how Edward was a fashion icon of the day, examining his clothes in Kensington Palace

programme.title: War Factories
programme.desc: How the Americans, once they were provoked into war, out-produced the rest of the world so quickly and by such a huge margin, including the likes of General Motors

programme.title: War Factories
programme.desc: Henry Ford's plan, with the help of Albert Kahn, to build a giant factory in Michigan, covering 3.5million sq ft and including an aircraft production line half a mile in length

programme.title: War Factories
programme.desc: The sabotaging efforts of Peugeot in Nazi-occupied France, where the bosses and workers cooperated closely with the Resistance to hinder the use of military vehicles by the Nazis

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Dave heads to West Yorkshire to collect a rare 1926 Dodge bus. Derek gets his hands on a rare Vauxhall Victor VX 90 police car that is expected to sell for more than a few coppers

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Dave heads to Bolton to pick up a classic Italian sports car - a Lancia Fulvia 1.3 Sport styled by Milan coachbuilder Zagato

programme.title: Restoration Workshop
programme.desc: Antiques dealer and expert restorer Gary Wallis and his team of highly skilled craftspeople turn the tired and unloved items into objects that will turn a large profit. In the opening episode, they restore a Grenadier guard's drum, create a garden feature from a helicopter tripod and re-purpose a vintage car grille into a gleaming design classic

programme.title: Restoration Workshop
programme.desc: Gary Wallis re-invents an old ships compass and a grand display cabinet into two of his most successful transformations to date

programme.title: Restoration Workshop
programme.desc: Gary Wallis visits an automobilia auction at Silverstone, creates a chair out of Second World War bomber jackets and gives a Victorian table a new zinc top

programme.title: Restoration Workshop
programme.desc: Gary Wallis visits Kempton Park Autojumble. He buys a tired vintage Moto Guzzi bike and transforms it into stunning display piece

programme.title: Wonders of the Universe
programme.desc: Professor Brian Cox explores how the development of mankind and the history of the universe are intertwined, visiting dramatic locations around the world to illustrate the fundamental scientific principles that govern the laws of nature. He begins by investigating time, charting the evolution of the cosmos and explaining why the universe must one day come to an end

programme.title: Wonders of the Universe
programme.desc: Professor Brian Cox continues his exploration of the origins of mankind by investigating the matter from which everything on Earth is formed. At a Hindu cremation in Nepal, he discusses the faith's philosophy of life as an eternal cycle of creation and destruction, and draws a parallel with the lives of the universe's stars - before revealing why everything on Earth was once a part of something else

programme.title: Wonders of the Universe
programme.desc: Professor Brian Cox explores how gravity influences events across the universe, discovering why such a relatively weak force dictates Earth's orbit around the Sun - as well as the solar system's movement through the galaxy. He also explains why the gravity of a neutron star is much stronger than that of Earth, and looks back through history to reveal why scientists' research into the power of attraction has given mankind a far greater understanding of the cosmos

programme.title: Wonders of the Universe
programme.desc: Professor Brian Cox reveals how the unique properties of light provide an insight into the origins and evolution of both mankind and the universe. He charts the importance of light in the development of the world's ancient civilisations, and explains how the speed of light allows the measurement of both distance and time - before pinpointing one of the most important moments in the evolution of life on Earth

programme.title: One Foot in the Grave
programme.desc: The Meldrews, Mrs Warboys and Nick Swainey spend a day boating on the river, but get marooned - so Victor sets out alone to fetch help and stumbles across a disturbing secret at an old people's home. Richard Wilson stars

programme.title: One Foot in the Grave
programme.desc: The Meldrews return home from a dreary holiday to find the neighbours about to sell their house to a weirdo - and while Victor is busy moaning about everything under the sun, Margaret considers embarking on an affair. Comedy, starring Richard Wilson and Annette Crosbie

programme.title: One Foot in the Grave
programme.desc: Victor is stuck at home while waiting to be called for jury service - but as time ticks on and his frustration grows, he begins to feel as if he is the one on trial. Richard Wilson stars in an episode featuring no other characters

programme.title: Guitar Heroes at the BBC
programme.desc: Archive footage from BBC music shows of the 1970s, including Pete Townshend of the Who, acoustic numbers by Davy Graham and Ralph McTell, and the talents of Americans Nils Lofgren and Ted Nugent. programme.title: Fleetwood Mac - Don't Stop

Mick Fleetwood, John McVie, Lindsey Buckingham and Stevie Nicks reveal the truth behind the successes, failures and relationships that characterised their music and made them one of the most enduring acts in popular music

programme.desc: Ninety-three-year-old D-Day veteran Thomas Hassell brings along a treasured item with a proud history - a smart military swagger stick awarded to him while on army service in Berlin for consistently being the smartest soldier on parade. The stick is a cherished symbol of a long and proud career, but sadly the decades have not been kind, and the silver head is badly dented and discoloured. It's down to silversmith Brenton West to knock out the dents and give the stick its swagger back. The team also restores a miniature whisky barrel and an antique set of scales

programme.title: Border Control: Spain
programme.desc: At the Port of Algeciras, a sniffer dog alerts officers to a suspicious truck. Plus, members of the Civil Guard question a suspected drug smuggler in Barcelona

programme.title: Border Control: Spain
programme.desc: At Barcelona Airport, a traveller reacts angrily when Civil Guard staff inspect her luggage. Meanwhile, officers uncover a large shipment of firearms

programme.title: Helicopter ER
programme.desc: The Yorkshire Air Ambulance crew is called to rescue a man trapped under a tractor that has plunged off a bridge and is in danger of sinking. Paramedic Paul is concerned his patient may go into cardiac arrest - and there's a real chance he may drown if firefighters cannot free him. The chopper lands on a farm to save a farmer who's been attacked by his own herd of cattle. Plus, on a road over the North York Moors, a wild deer runs out in front of a biker and leaves him with serious injuries

programme.title: Apollo: The Forgotten Films
programme.desc: Documentary charting an extensive search for unseen footage of the 1969 Moon landing, exploring the untold story behind these iconic images

programme.title: American Chopper
programme.desc: Paul Teutul Sr asks his son Paul Jr to look after the shop while he is away promoting his book, in a bid to keep the Mountain Creek project running smoothly

programme.title: Salvage Hunters: Best Restorations
programme.desc: Antiques expert Drew Pritchard looks back at the most incredible objects he gave to the restorers. They reveal how they gave new life to these items

programme.title: Mega Shippers
programme.desc: Massive machines are brought in to move a raised dual carriageway. Plus, a team races against time to underload a shipment of timber

programme.title: How It's Made
programme.desc: The making of turf grass, beef jerky, wood chippers and bowling pins

programme.title: How It's Made
programme.desc: The manufacture of items including Leatherman multi-tools, jojoba oil, and different kinds of marionette, and how processes have developed over the years

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final stay is at Delamere Farm near Peterborough, where proud owner Agi Chapman is sent into a spin after hearing some critical comments over breakfast

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Sussex, childhood sweethearts Jemma and James kick off with a menu based on their unlikely romance, finding a cold soup starter divides opinions. On day two, soon-to-be-retired dinner party veterans Phil and Sue cook up a menu of home comfort food, but disaster strikes in the dessert. On the final day, critical Steve and Aaron prepare a pescatarian feast with a fish-shaped salmon Wellington. Once again James and Jemma have to defend their relationship due to their less than romantic behaviour

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Tracey and Dean met in their early twenties and have devoted their lives to raising their family, including their own three children and two foster children. Now that they are finally in a position to buy a holiday home in the Mar Menor in Spain, Jasmine Harman is on hand to show them five properties for their £70,000 budget

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: After becoming foster parents to three young children, Jo Danson and her husband Bill decided a holiday home would be the perfect way for everyone to spend quality time together. Working within a budget of £65,000, Jasmine Harman shows Jo five suitable properties in the Orihuela Costa region of Spain

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin searches Lot-et-Garonne in south-west of France for Karen Wright and Philip Johnston, who dream of retiring to the country. They have £100,000, but that isn't just for them. The property needs to be large enough for their five dogs - and to accommodate even more as they have a dream of fostering rescue pooches

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Mother-of-three Virginia Houslin opens the contest in Huddersfield, West Yorkshire, and her hopes for some flirtatious fun are boosted by the arrival of her first guest, wedding singer Richard Wood. However the cheeky host faces a battle to win over the taste buds of her other diners as she tries to stake her claim for the £1,000 prize

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Business development manager Iyesha Atkins hosts the second dinner party in Huddersfield, West Yorkshire, hoping her sophisticated event will win her the £1,000 prize. However, the evening does not go to plan when Virginia finds fault with Iyesha's main course, and the two women fall out following a discussion about religion

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Wedding singer and confident cook Richard Wood hosts the third dinner party in Huddersfield. With steak on the menu, he opts not to listen to his guests' preferences and instead cooks and serves them his own way. However, with the starter a disaster and the rift between mum-of-three Virginia and ladette Iyesha deepening, Richard believes the only solution to save his night is for him to sing

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Tommy Mack hosts the fourth dinner-party challenge from Huddersfield, West Yorkshire, and tries to woo his guests with a Hollywood silent movie-themed evening, which includes a selection of mystery dishes and his very own style of rapping

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Kim Connor hosts the final party in Huddersfield, West Yorkshire, hoping her American diner-themed menu will delight her guests. Sweating over a hot stove is not what the glamorous hairdresser is used to, and she relies on helpful advice from her mother. The food fails to appeal to everyone's tastes, but Kim's after-dinner entertainment seems to revive her flagging chances of success

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay travels to the upmarket quarter of Hollywood to help the owner of failing health-food restaurant Sante La Brea. As well as struggling to control his disrespectful staff, the proprietor receives a surprise visit from the police at the height of dinner service

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Phillip aims to transform three derelict rooms at Chateau de la Fare into the perfect bridal suite for his daughter Penny's wedding in three months' time

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Danni Menzies helps Colin and Elspeth from Edinburgh search for a holiday home in Menorca, which they can enjoy well into their retirement with their family

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Jo and Darrell from south-west London are looking for a holiday home on the Balearic Island of Menorca to enjoy with their two young children and Darrell's grown-up son. They run their own busy human resources company together so cannot wait to find a bolthole to relax in and have some quality time as a family. With a budget of £300,000 Danni Menzies is on hand to show them five properties around the south of the island

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer look back at 20 years of trying to find homes for people with very specific criteria and must-haves. Ghika and Ronnie's search area is roughly two square miles, and they have been looking for the perfect house for a decade. Surfing is Jamie's passion, so he and wife Sam are looking for somewhere a stone's throw from the beach. Musicians Me'sha and Darren have viewed 70 properties but cannot seem to let go of anything on their wish list

programme.title: 999: What's Your Emergency?
programme.desc: The realities of policing home drinking, and what happens when parties go very badly wrong, seen through the eyes of South Yorkshire Police's frontline officers. PCs Alice Marshall and Matt Spaven investigate a house party that's a concern for neighbours, while PCs Mohammed Alrobaidi and Bernice Gott find themselves at risk at a rowdy house party in a quiet street. In Rotherham, PCs Pete Ellard and Tabitha Rumney have their hands full with a drunk and disorderly couple who are violently resisting arrest

programme.title: In the Footsteps of Killers
programme.desc: Emilia Fox and David Wilson investigate the 1967 murder of 19-year-old RAF servicewoman Rita Ellis, whose body was found by a dog walker near her base, having been killed in a brutal attack. Despite a number of leads the police have never managed to catch Rita's murderer and with a case that's 53 years old, Emilia and David have a challenge tracking down witnesses, other potential victims and a suspect

programme.title: Taken (2008)
programme.desc: An American teenager travelling through Europe is abducted by a gang of sex traffickers. However, what the kidnappers do not realise is that the girl's father is a former CIA agent, who employs all his old skills as he searches for the criminals and tries to rescue his daughter. Thriller, starring Liam Neeson, Famke Janssen and Maggie Grace

programme.title: Naked Attraction
programme.desc: A final romp through the Naked Attraction archive, remembering the times that the pickers got it spot on and chose their perfect date, leading to romance and happy endings. The success stories include Gemma and James, who found love on the show and whose relationship is still going strong

programme.title: Before We Die
programme.desc: Hannah and Billy know how the Mimicas plan to smuggle the drugs into the UK. But with Christian out of action, they have no clue when or where the consignment will be delivered. Meanwhile, when another drug dealer confesses to Sean's murder, it looks like the criminal family will get away with it - until Bianca makes a discovery that turns everything on its head

programme.title: Great Canal Journeys
programme.desc: Part one of two. Veteran actors and married couple Prunella Scales and Timothy West pass the tiller to Sheila Hancock and Gyles Brandreth, who are celebrating 40 years of friendship by making new memories together and learning new skills. Sheila and Gyles learn the ropes with Tim, and set out by themselves along the upper reaches of the River Thames. It's the same river that roars past their homes in London, but just 50 miles upstream it's an Arcadian paradise of unspoiled nature, royal retreats and river regattas

programme.title: Coast vs Country
programme.desc: Kerr Drummond shows Tom and Colette the very best of what their £1,200,000 budget can get them on the Dorset coast, while Kirsty Duffy takes them to view rural properties

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final day arrives, and the hosts of The White Horse Inn, Barnfield B&B, The Black Swan Inn and Delamere Farm all discover just how much their guests valued their services

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: On the first night in Hampshire, Serbian Stevan and his wife Clare serve up authentic Eastern European cuisine. Joining them to sample the delights of pickled cabbage are young students Josh and Bettie, while well-heeled James and Victoria bring a discerning eye to proceedings. On day two, youngsters Bettie and Josh are out to prove that there's more to student cooking than beans on toast. Finally, James and Victoria have chosen a menu themed around their favourite holiday destination of Spain

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Deon and Dave met in 2011 through the dating pages of a newspaper, and bonded over their shared love of the great outdoors and exploring different cultures. With a budget of £40,000, they're looking for somewhere bursting with Spanish character and charm and have Ben Hillman on hand to show them just how far their budget will stretch in the rural region of Jaen, Andalusia

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Having sold a successful business, Ruth and Philip Trinder from Northamptonshire want to buy an apartment on the Balearic island of Majorca, where they can spend half the year. They want somewhere they can entertain family and friends, within easy walking distance of one of the island's stylish ports. With a budget of £300,000 Laura Hamilton sets out to help them find something with the contemporary look they love

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Property expert Jasmine Harman helps Steph Fessey and Andy Wynn from Hampshire search for a property on Portugal's Silver Coast within a budget of £100,000. The couple are looking for a house with a spacious garden for when their children and grandchildren visit, as well as views of the countryside

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Ptol Slattery hosts the first dinner party in Winchester, Hampshire, but his menu of seared scallops, lamb steak with baby new potatoes followed by chocolate mousse leaves his guests - waitress Patrician Pearson, DJ Francis Leigh, care-home worker Georgia Groves and estate agent William Tilsley - underwhelmed

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: DJ Francis Leigh hosts the next dinner party in Winchester, hoping to dazzle his guests with his sunburnt chicken curry, a Thai meal with a twist and a dessert simply called 'sticky.' The entertainment for the night proves to be weird and wonderful as he dons a tutu and fur coat to serve the main course

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Waitress Patricia Pearson hosts the next party in Winchester, but the decision to write her menu in Spanish causes confusion for one guest who mistakes lamb for llama. The Peruvian-themed feast is followed by a rendition of the country's national anthem and a traditional dance involving a candle and a donkey's jaw

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: On the fourth night of the competition in Winchester, Georgia Groves plans a black-tie evening complete with a red carpet, posh frocks and a spray-tan booth. It appears to be going well until Patricia Pearson reveals what she thinks of Ptol Slattery and his wine antics

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Estate agent William Tilsley throws one last dinner party as a week of competitions in Winchester, Hampshire, draws to a close. The host plans to offer his guests a vegetarian menu, with a blind food-tasting game providing the evening's entertainment. As the night draws to a close, one of the competitors rises above the rest to become the winner

programme.title: Ramsay's 24 Hours to Hell and Back
programme.desc: Gordon travels to Greenville, Mississippi, to save fine dining establishment Sherman's Restaurant, which has been a presence in the town for 30 years. The chef and his team have just 24 hours to revamp the premises, clean and renovate the kitchen and introduce a new menu featuring update versions of the restaurant's staple dishes, working around the clock to teach the staff how to prepare them

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Phillip and Janet call on friends and family as they're down to the wire trying to finish the bridal suite in time for their daughter Penny's wedding. Mariam and Johnno get to work preparing all their bedrooms as they're due to host seven guests in three weeks' time. It's far from easy as Mariam struggles to find enough material to make curtains and finds out that a custom-made mattress doesn't fit an antique bed. Emma and Paul complete their Charles Rennie Mackintosh-themed bedroom in time for Emma's brother George's visit to celebrate his 60th birthday

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Jo and Darrell from south-west London are looking for a holiday home on the Balearic Island of Menorca to enjoy with their two young children and Darrell's grown-up son. They run their own busy human resources company together so cannot wait to find a bolthole to relax in and have some quality time as a family. With a budget of £300,000 Danni Menzies is on hand to show them five properties around the south of the island

programme.title: George Clarke's Amazing Spaces
programme.desc: Architect George Clarke delves into the world of small builds, meeting people taking tiny, unpromising spaces and creating innovative and exciting places to live, work and play. In the fourth episode of this ninth season, George is amazed by a couple's plan to turn a coach into a mobile home, complete with a garage for their treasured Mini. He also meets a pilot who has created a garden den from a small plane. Elsewhere, George's observatory build takes him to one of the world's largest telescopes

programme.title: George Clarke's Amazing Spaces
programme.desc: George travels to the Cornish village of Charlestown, where Charlotte and Joey have bought a public loo that they are planning to turn into a luxury clifftop holiday home - at a cost of £65,000. He also meets a Bristol family who are making their own safari tent, completes his tour of New Zealand with a visit to an underground house, and he and Will Hardie finally unveil their prototype futuristic home

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer look back at 20 years of trying to find homes for people with very specific criteria and must-haves. Ghika and Ronnie's search area is roughly two square miles, and they have been looking for the perfect house for a decade. Surfing is Jamie's passion, so he and wife Sam are looking for somewhere a stone's throw from the beach. Musicians Me'sha and Darren have viewed 70 properties but cannot seem to let go of anything on their wish list

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud follows the progress of Nathan and Amye as they build a giant cathedral-like property modelled on Dutch barn style houses, but with a contemporary twist. It is a hugely ambitious plan, and with Nathan project managing and setting themselves just a year to finish the build, the pressure to pull it off is huge

programme.title: Celebrity Gogglebox
programme.desc: Nick and Liv Grimshaw, Denise van Outen and Eddie Boxshall, Mo Gilligan and Babatunde Aleshe, Shaun Ryder and Bez, Martin and Roman Kemp, Gyles Brandreth and Maureen Lipman, Lorraine Kelly and Rosie Smith, Chris Eubank Sr and Chris Eubank Jr, and Eamonn Holmes and Ruth Langsford assess the week's week's biggest news stories, hard-hitting documentaries, laughable comedies and gritty dramas

programme.title: Celebrity Gogglebox
programme.desc: Nick and Liv Grimshaw, Denise van Outen and Eddie Boxshall, Mo Gilligan and Babatunde Aleshe, Shaun Ryder and Bez, Martin and Roman Kemp, Gyles Brandreth and Maureen Lipman, Iain Stirling and Laura Whitmore, Sunetra Sarker and Georgia Taylor, Lorraine Kelly and Rosie Smith, Melanie C and her brother Paul, Clare Balding and Alice Arnold, Chris Eubank Sr and Chris Eubank Jr, and Eamonn Holmes and Ruth Langsford offer sharp, insightful, funny and sometimes emotional critiques of popular and topical TV shows

programme.title: Dogging Tales
programme.desc: True Stories documentary by photographer Leo Maguire, providing an insight into why men and women engage in or watch sexual activity in front of strangers in public areas after dark. Accompanying his participants to lay-bys, woods and picnic spots around the UK, they discuss their attraction to dogging and reveal how they were introduced to it

programme.title: Bake Off: The Professionals
programme.desc: Tom Allen and Liam Charles present as the pastry chefs create miniature cone-shaped and carrot-shaped desserts - 24 of each. For the second challenge they must serve up a grand occasion showpiece - a Religieuse a l'Ancienne - to serve 24 people, with two different flavours of eclairs and inspired by the theme of time travel. Benoit Blin and Cherish Finden judge their efforts

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Noel treats chocolate Labrador Inca, who has a history of developmental elbow disease and osteoarthritis that has led to her elbow joints becoming worn out. Two-year-old Cavapoo Nala, who has hip dysplasia, and Great Dane Charles, who is unsteady on his feet due to a squashed spinal cord, are also on the patients list. Charles' owner Steve knows only too well that spinal surgery is a hugely serious undertaking, as he himself had multiple surgeries following a bike accident a decade earlier

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition kicks off at the White Horse Inn in Maldon, Essex, where owners Jayne and Pete Kocinoga hope their attention to detail will win over their guests

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit of the week is to Barnfield B&B in St Osyth, Essex, run by Christine and Paul, where as well as offering rooms, Christine also runs spiritual retreats

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: For the third visit of the week the contestants head to The Black Swan Inn in Pickering, Yorkshire, where husband and wife Phil and Jill Hall hope to impress their guests

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final stay is at Delamere Farm near Peterborough, where proud owner Agi Chapman is sent into a spin after hearing some critical comments over breakfast

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY

Mariam and Johnno transform their chateau's grand salon by installing reproduction Parquet de Versailles flooring and hand-painted murals. Plus, Karen and Paul aim to transform the old stable and coach house into a space fit for hosting weddings

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Jasmine Harman tries to help Dave and Debbie Gough from Swindon, who have been holidaying on the Costa Blanca for 30 years and now want to retire there

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Laura Hamilton helps Justine Gilbert find a new home on Florida's Gold Coast, so she can be closer to her two children who live in the US. programme.desc: Karen and Julian run a B&B in Cornwall and although they've never been to Naxos, they've decided it's the perfect place for a holiday home. Scarlette Douglas sets out to help them find the perfect pad on a budget of £215,000

A construction worker whose finger has been crushed discusses the importance of male role models in his life, and a haemophiliac reflects on living with his condition

programme.title: Celebrity Gogglebox
programme.desc: Nick and Liv Grimshaw, Denise van Outen and Eddie Boxshall, Mo Gilligan and Babatunde Aleshe, Shaun Ryder and Bez, Martin and Roman Kemp, Gyles Brandreth and Maureen Lipman, Lorraine Kelly and Rosie Smith, Chris Eubank Sr and Chris Eubank Jr, and Eamonn Holmes and Ruth Langsford assess the week's week's biggest news stories, hard-hitting documentaries, laughable comedies and gritty dramas

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Bart learns that he was once a child celebrity, but that Homer spent all the money he made, so he separates from his parents and moves into a loft apartment, where his neighbours are punk band Blink-182 and skateboarding hero Tony Hawk

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer develops a ravenous appetite for Krustyburger's new spare rib sandwich, while Lisa is offered a scholarship at the college of her choice - but only if she agrees to deliberately lose a spelling contest

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Glamorous Hollywood star Sara Sloane visits Springfield and becomes infatuated with Ned Flanders, who finds himself asking Homer for dating advice. Featuring the voices of Helen Fielding and Marisa Tomei

programme.title: Dance Moms
programme.desc: Abby Lee Miller looks back on the first seven years of the series as her time on the show nears its end, and reveals her creative inspirations

programme.title: Bargain-Loving Brits in the Sun
programme.desc: Horse-rescue boss Jill rushes to the aid of an animal abandoned at a roadside. Young couple Nicole and Aaron plan to buy and refurbish a campervan so they can travel the world putting on fitness boot camps. Former call-centre employee Courtney and her boyfriend Michael get the chance to run their own bar, although their boss is soon piling on the pressure to get it open as soon as possible

programme.title: Rich House, Poor House
programme.desc: Two more families swap homes, budgets and lifestyles for a week to see how the other half lives. For Matthew and Belinda Sethard-Wright and their two children, this means exchanging their large house in Orpington for a Bromley council house. Moving into their home will be Skye Walker and her three children. As a professional housekeeper, Skye works in houses not unlike the Sethard-Wrights'. Now she will be able to enjoy its many comforts for herself - include a swimming pool, tennis court and weekly £1,500 shopping budget

programme.title: Filthy Britain SOS
programme.desc: A super sleuth attempts to bring down a seven-and-a-half-ton fly-by-night dumper, proving that cameras never lie. And in Braintree, Essex, former military man Stu is out to blanket his entire county in hidden CCTV, in order to catch the culprits plaguing his patch

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: Officers tail a Renault linked to a gang-related stabbing in Derby, but as they try to make a stop, the driver abandons the car and makes a run for it. The Road Crime Units gather intel on a suspect vehicle believed to be used for dealing drugs on a Chesterfield estate, while police track down a car with links to an alleged knifepoint robbery at a suspected drug dealer's house

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: Yorkshire police forces tackling an ever-more sophisticated criminal fraternity from the cities to the Dales. Police from the north and west of the historic county crack down on drug-addled drivers, and it is North Yorkshire's Roads Policing Group that faces the toughest challenge when a convicted drug dealer leads officers on a 140mph chase on one of the UK's busiest motorways. Jamie Theakston narrates

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Jason heads to a Christmas party that has turned into a full-blown riot, while Adam and Nick catch a drunken woman giving her daughter a lift to the shops. Plus, Kev is seconds away from disaster when he places himself in the path of danger to prevent a car from becoming a fireball

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: A runaway suspect puts Gary's life in danger, Scott and Adam discover a huge stash of drugs in the boot of a car, and officers are called to a New Year's Eve pub brawl. Plus, Ady catches a car thief, and five police vehicles are involved in a motorway chase

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Scott and Dave use GPS technology to track a pair of mobile-phone thieves, and a car crashes into a house during the Christmas period. Plus, Nick pursues a criminal gang, Vicky deals with a van containing suspicious-looking scrap, and Jason contends with his own traffic nightmare

programme.title: Dogs Behaving (Very) Badly
programme.desc: Master dog trainer Graeme Hall finds long lasting solutions for dogs with problems. They include Butty, who is bullying the female members of his household, and Pixie, who howls like a banshee the moment she sees another dog. Cameras also follow Lucy, who will stay in the park for hours to raid picnics

programme.title: Neighbours
programme.desc: Toadie is furious that Melanie's squabble with Angela put Hugo in danger. Terese and Paul pressure Amy to keep the Flamingo Bar's profits up over winter

programme.title: Home and Away
programme.desc: Jasmine is thrilled that she got the nursing unit position, but Tori does not share her joy. Roo tries to convince Alf that he cannot keep working three jobs

programme.title: Bargain-Loving Brits in the Sun
programme.desc: Expats Russell and Lindsay hope to attract punters to their convenience store with a selection of pastries, while retired nurse Susie and her husband Reg, who volunteer at a dog shelter, have their hands full with a littler of 10 puppies. Life coach Svetlana's online rants about the behaviour of expats on the Costa del Sol have gone viral. But as she prepares to record another monologue, she has a surprise up her sleeve

programme.title: Casualty 24/7: Every Second Counts
programme.desc: Documentary following the work of medical emergency teams in the A&E department of Barnsley Hospital. 18-year-old Dalton is assessed by Nurse Boothroyd as he is struggling to breathe, and 32-year-old Michael is rushed in after being involved in a serious motorbike accident. Elsewhere, a 27-year-old patient with a rare condition needs special attention

programme.title: 999: Critical Condition
programme.desc: Trauma team leader Julie Norton battles to save two patients whose lives hang in the balance. Father of four Ian is rushed in after suffering a cardiac arrest, while another man is bleeding so heavily he risks losing his entire circulatory system. Meanwhile, Ken has fallen from a roof onto a skip, leaving him with a gaping wound to his thigh and potentially life-threatening injuries to his pelvis and spine, and Dr Nayak helps Michael, who has suffered a severe stroke

programme.title: Extraordinary People
programme.desc: In 2010, US marine sergeant John Peck lost his arms and legs while serving in Afghanistan. Dissatisfied with his prosthetics and determined to regain his independence, he put himself forward for ground-breaking surgery. This documentary follows John as he undergoes a bilateral arm transplant in an extraordinary 10-hour operation with 20 surgical staff in attendance

programme.title: The Craft (1996)
programme.desc: A new girl who finds it difficult to fit in at school befriends three goth misfits, who discover they can perform magic and form a secret coven. They initially use their powers for self-improvement, but eventually start to turn against their classmates, driving the ringleader insane as her lust for power takes over. Horror, starring Robin Tunney, Fairuza Balk, Neve Campbell, Rachel True and Skeet Ulrich

programme.title: Bargain-Loving Brits in the Sun
programme.desc: Christian and Mandy, the organisers of the annual Pride festival, are 4,000 euros short of the 18,000 required to stage the weekend-long party - and if that were not bad enough, the costumes are seven sizes too big. With two weeks left, can the duo save the day? Cameras also follow bar owner Cath as she prepares to host a hen party for 14 excited Northern Irish women and Leeds lad Peter, who publishes a free pocket guide to the Costa del Sol

programme.title: Rich House, Poor House
programme.desc: Single mum Claire Lee swaps her three-bedroom Wolverhampton terraced house to spend a week in the nearby five-bedroom home of cardiologist and paediatrician Mahrous and Rana Alsoud - and vice versa - to see how the other half lives. Claire and her five children will see their weekly budget increase from £171 to a massive £3,000, while Rana is hoping the swap will give her children a taste of her own upbringing in Syria

programme.title: Filthy Britain SOS
programme.desc: Following Hampshire's volunteer waste champions, who are using drone technology to collar the rogue waste operators upsetting their patch

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: West Yorkshire officers take action against a teenage driver after he leads them on a high-speed chase across Roundhay Park in Leeds, while the cops also face a battle to keep up with uninsured drivers who flout the law without fear of consequence

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: The officers deal with a number of uninsured drivers, including one who leads them on a 35-minute chase and another who flees the scene of a rush-hour crash, earning himself a full-scale investigation for his trouble. Then in Bradford, a crackdown on road offences leads to the officers nabbing another insurance avoider, but tensions run high as a result

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Documentary following the work of high-speed interception teams, this time with access to the officers of Cumbria's Roads Policing Unit. Glen has a busy night in Barrow-in-Furness dealing with a drunk and a runaway knifeman, and Colin detains a suspected drug dealer who has a wrap of heroin hidden in a very intimate place. Countryside and city collide when Duggie has to tackle a trailer full of cows that has overturned, blocking the main carriageway into Carlisle, while the alcohol-fuelled actions of one driver send officers rushing to evacuate villagers in the middle of the night after a crash ruptures a gas main

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: A stick of dynamite causes chaos for Duggie when it is brought into Carlisle's main police station and the armed response unit is called out after there is a report of a gun being spotted in a city centre bar. Duncan receives a shock when a cyclist who has been trapped under a car turns out to be someone he knows, officers haul in a driver who is well over the limit and Hayley comes to the rescue when a teenager's car hits a wall during a late-night drive. Documentary following the work of Cumbria's Roads Policing Unit

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Ross and John put their armed-response training to the test when they tackle a suspected gunman, while teams are called out to deal with the results of dangerous driving after a person parks on a roundabout and a pedestrian is crushed under a car. Elsewhere, officers raid a cannabis farm, a Reliant Robin is bid a fiery farewell, and Phil finds out what happens when the driver of a Subaru Impreza decides to take on an HGV

programme.title: Dogs Behaving (Very) Badly
programme.desc: Graeme Hall continues to find solutions for dogs with problems. After a break-up, Hampshire woman Rebecca leaned on her Manchester terrier Marney for support. But now Marney will not let anyone new into her owner's life. In London, Jasper and Alexis cannot live a normal life because their Irish terrier Albert guards the front door like a bouncer

programme.title: Neighbours
programme.desc: Roxy's attempts at sabotage lead to a serious safety risk. Levi pushes himself too hard while trying to distract himself from his broken heart by working out

programme.title: Home and Away
programme.desc: Mackenzie clashes with Chloe and Ryder over the location of their food truck. Justin tries to shift the blame for his erratic behaviour onto those around him

programme.title: Filthy House SOS
programme.desc: Richard and Brennan come to the aid of a single father in Keighley, who has two sons, one dog, two cats and a pair of parrots to take care of - leaving little time for housework

programme.title: Rich Holiday, Poor Holiday
programme.desc: Two wealthy friends from London used to the most glamorous luxury getaways all over the world swap holidays with a woman and her three children from East Lothian who are unable to afford to all go abroad together. Julia and Katya head off to Tenerife for two nights in a resort hostel, followed by a working break at the island's horse rescue centre, while Annie and her adult children have a week at a luxury historic mansion hotel in the Mediterranean resort of Antalya in Turkey

programme.title: Red 2 (2013)
programme.desc: Former CIA agent Frank Moses reunites his squad of retired operatives for a vital mission. A devastatingly powerful new weapon has been stolen, and it falls to Frank's team to retrieve it - which means facing lethal assassins, terrorists and corrupt government officials out to steal the device. Action thriller sequel, starring Bruce Willis, Helen Mirren and John Malkovich

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote
programme.desc: Jessica pulls out all the stops to help private eye Harry McGraw find his partner's murderer. Angela Lansbury and Jerry Orbach star

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Assistant coroner Palmer is called to the building site on a Navy base where a body has been found. While looking into the incident, he spots an armed man fleeing the scene with the victim's passport and is almost killed as he gives chase. It appears the document was stolen to hinder the investigation, but the discovery of a second corpse reveals a case of stolen identity and a compensation racket

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: In the first of a two-part story, the entire team is put in danger while hunting for the killer of a former NCIS agent. Jenny Shepard calls in Mike Franks for help, afraid she too is a target - and convinced Gibbs will be next. American crime drama, starring Mark Harmon and Michael Weatherly

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: A man is found murdered in a local park and the death is initially linked to drugs, but as Bernard and Lupo investigate they discover fingerprints at the scene that lead them to a man with learning difficulties who holds the key to the killing

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Lupo and Bernard try to solve the murder of a 17-year-old youth, and discover his younger brother had recently been banished from a polygamous Mormon sect. As they investigate the group's controversial leader, the detectives learn he had been trying to track down one of his wives. Crime drama, starring Jeremy Sisto, Anthony Anderson and Sam Waterston, with Colm Meaney (Star Trek: The Next Generation)

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Paramedics become suspicious about a baby's death, calling in Briscoe and Curtis, who discover the parents adopted the child from an illegal Russian ring. The case takes on another dimension altogether when it transpires that the crooks specialise in placing terminally ill infants with unsuspecting families. Starring Jerry Orbach and Benjamin Bratt

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Lupo and Bernard investigate the death of a troubled NYPD officer, a man with mounting financial difficulties who was killed by colleagues after taking hostages at gunpoint. As the pair dig deeper into his life, they discover a stash of valuable documents in his apartment as well as a body, and the evidence leads to a man claiming to be the illegitimate son of a famous president

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: An investigation is launched when a war widow with a possible grudge against the army is found unconscious in the rubble of a bombed recruitment centre alongside her lifeless child. Lupo and Bernard begin their search for the bomber and encounter a number of suspects, including a man whose sister was killed in Iraq and the leader of a group of war protesters

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: The execution-style murder of a prison guard triggers an examination of the corruption apparently inherent in the state's penal system. Police drama, starring Sam Waterston and Angie Harmon

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Part two of two. Vance sticks rigidly to the rules as he investigates the death of a fellow agent, but Gibbs and Franks take matters into their own hands to catch the killers. The case also leads to major changes for the agency. Drama, starring Rocky Carroll, Mark Harmon and Muse Watson

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs discovers a link between the murder of a petty officer and the break-up of his team, leading him and new director Leon Vance to uncover a case of blackmail involving an NCIS agent. The duo single out three suspects, but learn vital information has been stolen, and realise the whole Persian Gulf Fleet could be in danger

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A lieutenant apparently commits suicide by jumping from an aircraft carrier and Ziva and McGee are dispatched to inform the widow - but arrive to find that she has been killed. Subsequent inquiries reveal that the injuries on her body were inflicted after death and the murder scene was carefully staged to mislead investigators. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon and Cote de Pablo

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: Benson investigates a police officer's sexual assault case, while Tucker's retirement party ends in disaster. Crime drama, starring Abby Royle and Mariska Hargitay

programme.title: Law & Order: Criminal Intent
programme.desc: An arrogant gossip columnist is murdered, and Logan enlists the help of Megan Wheeler, back in the US after working undercover in Europe, to help him investigate a related celebrity sex scandal. However, as the duo scrutinise the dead man's life, the list of possible suspects grows increasingly longer

programme.title: Law & Order: Criminal Intent
programme.desc: A famous crime writer who used to date Captain Ross asks him to look into the disappearance of her much younger husband. Goren and Eames trace the man's movements via his car's GPS tracking system and suspect he was the victim of a carjacking. Further inquires lead them to believe he had staged the crime to run off with his lover, who is subsequently reported missing by her parents. Guest starring Brenda Strong (Desperate Housewives) and Eric Roberts (Heroes)

programme.title: Law & Order: Criminal Intent
programme.desc: Broadway star Marc Landry falls to his death after his flying harness fails during a performance of a play based on the Greek myth of Icarus. Goren and Eames are called in when it becomes clear the rigging had been tampered with, and the detectives talk to director Amanda Rollins about the significance of a bouquet of flowers with peacock feathers delivered to the victim. Goren also pays a visit to old friend and mythology lecturer Cleo Andrews for expert advice. Guest starring Cynthia Nixon and Patti Smith

programme.title: Law & Order: Criminal Intent
programme.desc: In the last-ever episode of the long-running crime drama, twins Parker and Thomas Gaffney are found dead in the offices of a successful internet dating website. It emerges they believed the company's owner Danielle Magee had stolen their business idea after they employed her as a programmer and had filed a lawsuit against her. Goren attends his final therapy session and finds his future in the force is in question. Vincent D'Onofrio stars

programme.title: Castle
programme.desc: The crime drama returns with a call from a mysterious stranger igniting an explosive chain of events, with the first of a two-part story told from Castle's perspective as he searches for his Beckett. Nathan Fillion and Stana Katic star

programme.title: Castle
programme.desc: Part two of two. After receiving a mysterious tip from someone from her past, Beckett is launched into a fight for her life with a team of mercenaries hot on her trail. While Castle investigates why his wife is on the run, Beckett must figure out who wants her dead before they can finish the job

programme.title: Criminals: Caught on Camera
programme.desc: Journalist Nick Wallis joins police forces around the nation to discover how CCTV and technological advances are helping to bring criminals to justice. In this edition, he introduces footage of reckless acts behind the wheel by criminals and other drivers exhibiting a complete disregard for the safety of others

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Lupo and Bernard investigate when a man is crushed to death under a crane. Initially they suspect the construction company of foul play, but the case leads them to the victim's hospitalised wife, as well as plans for a controversial medical procedure

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Lupo and Bernard investigate the murder of a man visiting New York City to confront his past, but as they delve into the victim's personal life, the case becomes complicated when all clues point to a revered sheriff. Meanwhile, McCoy faces competition when a new district attorney is nominated for election

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: McCoy and Carmichael have the unenviable task of prosecuting three police officers with a racially motivated hate crime that threatens to cause uproar in the city. Police drama, starring Sam Waterston and Angie Harmon

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: The murder of a religious website creator leads Bernard and Lupo to a network of corruption in a charitable foundation that provides the motive for the crime. The case becomes even more complicated when the suspect manages to avoid prosecution by being granted asylum, leaving McCoy fighting for his career

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Bernard and Lupo investigate the suspected hit-and-run death of the mistress of the CEO of a failed investment bank. The evidence leads the pair to a kidnapping and extortion plot that uncovers several potential suspects, including a disgruntled client

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: The detectives investigate the double murder of a wealthy man and his daughter, and the evidence leads them to suspect the wife and stepmother of the deceased are guilty

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs receives a call from his old friend Senator Patrick Kiley, who claims he is being framed for the murder of a naval commander, and seeks help to clear his name. However, incriminating evidence quickly piles up against him - and a second death complicates matters further. Meanwhile, Ziva lets her hair down to obtain fingerprints from a big oil lobbyist. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs revisits his home town to investigate an assault that left one marine dead and another badly injured, and enlists the help of his estranged father to identify the assailant. Suspicion initially falls on a local man whose daughter was dating the survivor of the attack - but later inquiries uncover a tangled tale of uncertain paternity and forbidden love. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon and Ralph Waite

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A marine is found murdered in his garage, and inquiries lead the agents to his former sergeant. However, Gibbs and the team soon find out that their man is under FBI witness protection as he is about to testify against a mobster, giving him a cast-iron alibi. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon and Joe Spano

programme.title: Columbo: Double Shock (1973)
programme.desc: A wealthy man due to marry a younger woman is electrocuted in the bath. Columbo suspects the victim's identical twin nephews - a flamboyant TV chef and a conservative banker - who stand to inherit his fortune if he died unmarried, but the detective is unsure which of them is the guilty party. Drama, starring Peter Falk, and Martin Landau in a dual role. With Paul Stewart, Julie Newmar and Jeanette Nolan

programme.title: Columbo: Murder Under Glass (1978)
programme.desc: The crumpled detective applies his well-honed instincts to a case of blackmail and murder in the world of haute cuisine when a restaurant owner is found dead after throwing a renowned food critic off his premises. Crime drama from The Silence of the Lambs director Jonathan Demme, starring Peter Falk, Louis Jourdan, Shera Danese and Richard Dysart

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: Benson puts herself in danger when a home invasion escalates into a hostage situation

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: A high-school rape accusation escalates into a complicated 'he-said-she-said' debacle, and with no conclusive or satisfactory resolution on the horizon, it becomes unclear what - if anything - has taken place. Meanwhile, getting embroiled in an ugly legal battle threatens the futures of two promising students. Legal drama, starring Mariska Hartigay

programme.title: Castle

programme.desc: Rollins leads a sting operation to take down child pornographer Teddy Hawkins, also well-known as the owner of a chain of successful restaurants. With two high-profile arrests to their name, Rollins and her colleagues must work quickly to justify their actions before Abraham uses his power to make the charges 'go away'. Crime drama, starring Kelli Giddish

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: The team investigates when Libby Parker is followed home by a gang of teenagers, and raped in the courtyard of her apartment block. The prosecutors face an even more difficult dilemma when it emerges none of Libby's neighbours are willing to make a statement either. Crime drama, starring Mariska Hartigay

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: Notorious serial killer Gregory Williams Yeats concocts an elaborate plan to free himself from incarceration by co-operating with one-time nemesis Dr Carl Rudnick. programme.desc: The New York police drama returns for its tenth and final series. A woman is found shot dead two days after attending a party thrown by fashion designer Nyle Brite. A search of her home uncovers a large selection of wigs, condoms and clothes by the designer, plus a number of nasty text messages on her phone, leading the team to wonder whether she moonlighted as a high-class prostitute. Crime drama, starring Vincent D'Onofrio and Kathryn Erbe

programme.title: Law & Order: Criminal Intent

Crime drama, starring Kathryn Erbe and Vincent D'Onofrio

programme.title: Stargate SG-1
programme.desc: Daniel has difficulty convincing the rest of the team that a mysterious crystal skull has the power to transport humans to areas inhabited by aliens. Sci-fi drama, starring Michael Shanks

programme.title: Stargate Atlantis
programme.desc: Sheppard is captured by a race of human travellers that has lived for generations in space to avoid the Wraith's systematic culls. Believing the Atlantis expedition to be a powerful enemy of the Wraith, they intend to use his genes to reactivate a long-dormant Ancient warship. Guest starring Jill Wagner

programme.title: Star Trek: The Next Generation
programme.desc: Picard is compelled to accept a stubborn officer's assistance in tracking down a Bajoran terrorist leader blamed for an attack on a Federation colony - but soon discovers his superiors have a hidden agenda. Guest starring Whoopi Goldberg and Michelle Forbes

programme.title: Star Trek: Enterprise
programme.desc: The crew members are ambushed by a hostile Klingon when they board a stricken vessel, and find themselves stranded on the floundering ship. Sci-fi drama, starring Scott Bakula and Jolene Blalock

programme.title: Star Trek: Deep Space Nine
programme.desc: A Bajoran village is attacked by a mysterious destructive energy force. O'Brien beams down in answer to the community's emergency call and is shocked to find himself appointed their new leader. Starring Avery Brooks and Colm Meaney

programme.title: Hawaii Five-0
programme.desc: Tani, Junior and several civilians are trapped when a tunnel collapses, while Eddie the dog sniffs out a bomb that has been planted in McGarrett's home

programme.title: The Force: Manchester
programme.desc: Officers raid a cannabis farm and police are called to a fight in a pub car park. Documentary following the work of Greater Manchester Police

programme.title: Brit Cops

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: The story of Diane Downs, the Arizona-born mother of three who in 1983 arrived at hospital, having murdered one of her children and left her remaining two critically injured

programme.title: Fred Dinenage: Murder Casebook
programme.desc: Fred Dinenage offers an insight into the life of Lord Lucan, suspected of murdering his children's nanny, Sandra Rivett, in November 1974. The peer subsequently disappeared and has never been seen since, though unsubstantiated rumours suggest he died in Africa in 2004

programme.title: The Force: Manchester

Timing is everything, too, when the crew turn their attention to a four-year-old girl with a serious leg fracture

programme.title: Whatever Love Means ()
programme.desc: Drama chronicling the passionate relationship between Prince Charles and Camilla Parker Bowles prior to his marriage to Diana, Princess of Wales, in 1981. The film recreates the couple's first meeting at a polo match in 1971 and reveals how the confident 23-year-old Camilla Shand made a lasting impression on the prince. Although their friendship was fuelled by a shared sense of humour, Camilla's on-off affair with Andrew Parker Bowles meant there could be no future for them as man and wife. Camilla even suggested potential suitors as the pressure grew for the heir to the throne to find a suitable partner. Starring Laurence Fox, Olivia Poulet, Richard Johnson and Michelle Duncan

programme.title: Brakes (2016)
programme.desc: Comedy outlining the seemingly random and fragile nature of the connections that can lead to people becoming part of a couple, showing the final stages of a number of relationships then flashing back to how they got together. Starring Julian Barratt, Kerry Fox and Paul McGann

programme.title: Strike It Lucky
programme.desc: Michael Barrymore hosts the madcap game show, in which three teams of two prove seek to progress along a board game-style path by answering questions. Each duo are allowed to determine how many spaces they would like to move by answering the corresponding number of questions. Landing on a space reveals it's hidden contents, which could amount to a prize, or a 'hot spot', which causes contestants to forfeit their gathered winnings
